name: Team Cooper
subject: Recapping the last 30-ish days ⏪
body:
New digital scrapbook just dropped.
|
# [](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1898?t=1&akid=109%2E124072%2EldwlMG)
Good evening, Peter! Team Cooper here. Wherever you are, we hope you had a safe, warm, and restful Thanksgiving. This holiday, it was our pleasure to spotlight the **[Food Bank of Central& Eastern North Carolina](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2136?t=7&akid=109%2E124072%2EldwlMG)** as they do important work to keep folks from going hungry — especially around the holidays. If you made a donation or volunteered your time, _thank you._ Keep reading for some updates from the campaign trail. Think of it like a digital scrapbook of the last 30-ish days, from us to you! 👇
* * *
**ROY ON THE ROAD** [](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2137?t=8&akid=109%2E124072%2EldwlMG) _Roy hears it from North Carolinians everywhere he goes — people are struggling because**there’s too much month at the end of the money.** MAGA Republicans have pushed food prices through the roof. Folks who work for a living are paying for it while billionaires get another tax break. Roy’s running to change that._ **[Roy spoke about affordability at the grocery store, health care costs, what it takes to win in North Carolina, and more with MeidasTouch — watch the full interview here!](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2137?t=9&akid=109%2E124072%2EldwlMG)** [](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2138?t=10&akid=109%2E124072%2EldwlMG) _It was a true honor for our campaign to spend Veterans Day in Fayetteville with the brave men and women who have served this country. We owe them a deep debt of gratitude._
* * *
**HAPPY 2ND BIRTHDAY, NC MEDICAID EXPANSION! 🎂** [](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2139?t=11&akid=109%2E124072%2EldwlMG) Two years ago, Roy’s administration did what everyone said was impossible: Expanded Medicaid. It was a decade-long battle that was well worth it to get quality, affordable health care to **more than 690,000 North Carolinians**. Roy worked with _everyone_ — Republicans and Democrats alike — to get it done. [](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2140?t=12&akid=109%2E124072%2EldwlMG) Medicaid Expansion helped hardworking North Carolinians across the state access care and stay healthy, particularly in rural areas. Now, that progress is at risk. Extreme DC Republicans like Michael Whatley are trying to rip away health care from millions of people. Roy is running to stop them. **[Has Medicaid expansion helped you or someone you know? Share your story!](https://act.roycooper.com/survey/medicaid-expansion-poll/?source=em_RC_OTH_NEWSLETTER_120525&t=13&akid=109%2E124072%2EldwlMG)** | [YES](https://act.roycooper.com/survey/medicaid-expansion-poll/?source=em_RC_OTH_NEWSLETTER_120525&t=14&akid=109%2E124072%2EldwlMG) | [NO](https://act.roycooper.com/survey/medicaid-expansion-poll/?source=em_RC_OTH_NEWSLETTER_120525&t=15&akid=109%2E124072%2EldwlMG)
---|---
* * *
**GOOD NEWS: MEDICAL DEBT RELIEF SHATTERS EXPECTATIONS 📈**
It’s a pleasure to share some more good news with you: Thanks to the Medical Debt Relief Program that Governor Cooper signed into law, North Carolina has **erased more than $6.5 billion in medical debt for more than 2.5 million North Carolinians (and counting)!**
This is well beyond the initial projections, and it’s why, as governor, Roy put this plan into action. **Making everyday life easier and more affordable for North Carolinians is always the top priority.**
* * *
**MAGA MICHAEL MISSES THE MARK ❌**
[](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2142?t=16&akid=109%2E124072%2EldwlMG)
_Michael Whatley claims grocery prices are down. He obviously hasn’t been in a Harris Teeter, a Food Lion, or an Ingles lately! NC deserves a Senator who’ll tell you the truth — not parrot MAGA Republican talking points_
* * *
In case you missed it: **We have a new phone number!** 📲 Make sure you’re getting our messages by **texting JOIN to 56801.**
---
* * *
**THE MERCH STORE IS OPEN!**
[](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2143?t=17&akid=109%2E124072%2EldwlMG)
_We launched our campaign store just in time for the holidays. If you want to give some Cooper for North Carolina gifts this holiday season,**[get them here!](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2143?t=18&akid=109%2E124072%2EldwlMG)** (Place your order for standard items by 12/8 to receive it in time for Christmas!)_
That’s all for now. See you again next time!
— Team Cooper
**FOLLOW ALONG:**
**[](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2019?t=19&akid=109%2E124072%2EldwlMG)**| **[](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2020?t=20&akid=109%2E124072%2EldwlMG)**| **[](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2021?t=21&akid=109%2E124072%2EldwlMG)**| **[](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2022?t=22&akid=109%2E124072%2EldwlMG)**| **[](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2023?t=23&akid=109%2E124072%2EldwlMG)**
---|---|---|---|---
* * *
Democrat Roy Cooper is running for U.S. Senate to help take back the Majority for Democrats. North Carolina is Democrats’ best chance to flip a seat from red to blue — the path to stopping Republicans in Washington starts here.

National Republicans will target North Carolina as a key battleground and invest heavily in defeating us.

Cooper is a former state legislator, Attorney General, and most recently Governor of North Carolina. North Carolina will be the most competitive Senate election of 2026, and Cooper enters the race as the most formidable candidate of either party.

Cooper’s signature achievements include helping to expand Medicaid and helping make NC the top state for business by creating thousands of jobs and growing the clean energy economy.

**[Donate today to help win this seat and flip the Senate blue.](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1914?t=2&akid=109%2E124072%2EldwlMG)**
[DONATE](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1914?t=3&akid=109%2E124072%2EldwlMG)
---
[DONATE MONTHLY](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1915?t=4&akid=109%2E124072%2EldwlMG)
[SHOP OUR MERCH STORE](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2133?t=5&akid=109%2E124072%2EldwlMG)
[info@roycooper.com](mailto:info@roycooper.com) | [Privacy Policy](https://act.roycooper.com/go/825?t=6&akid=109%2E124072%2EldwlMG)
Cooper Victory Fund
PO Box 1190
Raleigh, NC 27602
Paid for by Cooper Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by Cooper for North Carolina, DSCC, and North Carolina Democratic Party — Federal.
---
Contributions or gifts to Cooper Victory Fund are not tax deductible.
The first $7,000 of each contribution from a person and the first $10,000 from a multicandidate committee (“PAC”) will be allocated to Cooper for North Carolina. The first $3,500 of a person’s contribution and the first $5,000 of a PAC’s contribution will be designated for the primary election; the next $3,500/$5,000 will be designated for the general election. The next $10,000 from a person and $5,000 from a PAC will be allocated to North Carolina Democratic Party – Federal. The next $44,300 of a contribution from a person and the next $15,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC General Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Headquarters Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Legal Fund. A contributor may designate his or her contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if following it would result in an excessive contribution.
Any donor history information in this email reflects what we have on file for this specific email address. If you have donated with a different email address, with a check, or with a spouse — thank you so much! We have that on file and cannot thank you enough for supporting this campaign.
By clicking the links above, you certify that you are responsible for paying all charges incurred in using the debit or credit card to be charged, and that your personal funds will be the true source of the contribution.

[You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time.](http://act.roycooper.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=109%2E124072%2EldwlMG)
If you wish to receive fewer messages from us, [submit this form to let us know.](https://act.roycooper.com/signup/i-want-less-emails-cooper-north-carolina/?t=24&akid=109%2E124072%2EldwlMG)

NC deserves a Senator who’ll tell you the truth — not parrot MAGA Republican talking points In case you missed it: We have a new phone number! 📲 Make sure you’re getting our messages by texting JOIN to 56801. THE MERCH STORE IS OPEN! We launched our campaign store just in time for the holidays. If you want to give some Cooper for North Carolina gifts this holiday season, (Place your order for standard items by 12/8 to receive it in time for Christmas!) That’s all for now. See you again next time! — Team Cooper FOLLOW ALONG: | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- Democrat Roy Cooper is running for U.S. Senate to help take back the Majority for Democrats. North Carolina is Democrats’ best chance to flip a seat from red to blue — the path to stopping Republicans in Washington starts here. National Republicans will target North Carolina as a key battleground and invest heavily in defeating us. Cooper is a former state legislator, Attorney General, and most recently Governor of North Carolina. North Carolina will be the most competitive Senate election of 2026, and Cooper enters the race as the most formidable candidate of either party. Cooper’s signature achievements include helping to expand Medicaid and helping make NC the top state for business by creating thousands of jobs and growing the clean energy economy. ** mailto:info@roycooper.com | Cooper Victory Fund PO Box 1190 Raleigh, NC 27602 Paid for by Cooper Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by Cooper for North Carolina, DSCC, and North Carolina Democratic Party — Federal. Contributions or gifts to Cooper Victory Fund are not tax deductible. The first $7,000 of each contribution from a person and the first $10,000 from a multicandidate committee (“PAC”) will be allocated to Cooper for North Carolina. The first $3,500 of a person’s contribution and the first $5,000 of a PAC’s contribution will be designated for the primary election; the next $3,500/$5,000 will be designated for the general election. The next $10,000 from a person and $5,000 from a PAC will be allocated to North Carolina Democratic Party – Federal. The next $44,300 of a contribution from a person and the next $15,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC General Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Headquarters Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Legal Fund. A contributor may designate his or her contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if following it would result in an excessive contribution. Any donor history information in this email reflects what we have on file for this specific email address. If you have donated with a different email address, with a check, or with a spouse — thank you so much! We have that on file and cannot thank you enough for supporting this campaign. Over the past three years, I’ve tried to honor your trust by showing up, listening, and fighting for things that actually make life better here at home. From lowering everyday costs, to strengthening veterans’ services, supporting childcare access, protecting our forests, and making sure Congress works for the people again – I’m still in this fight.

But this next year will be tougher than anything we’ve faced. WA-03 is already in the national spotlight, and what we do now will determine whether we enter 2026 on solid footing.

People across Northern California stand with Mike because he’s shown his **commitment to delivering on affordable healthcare, reducing the costs of living, protecting our environment, and so much more.** He knows how to get the job done and he isn’t beholden to anyone.

name: Team Fetterman
subject: Trump's toxic chemical cleanup delay
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fetterman for PA
body:
| | |
---
**_BREAKING → _Trump Administration DELAYING toxic chemical cleanups across the country**
Derek, we're not talking small delays either – in some cases, they're nearly a **decade**.

**Right here in PA, three bases are facing delays of up to five years. That’s unacceptable. 🤬**
PFAS exposure is tied to cancers, weakened immune systems, birth defects – a whole list of serious health threats. And because PFAS-containing firefighting foam was used for decades on military sites, these chemicals have seeped straight into the drinking water of service members, their families, and surrounding communities.
These families don’t have years to wait while Trump focuses on what he apparently considers more important – like starting trade wars or passing tax cuts for the ultrawealthy. 🙄 **Their health is at risk _right now._**
That’s why John joined 27 Senate colleagues in demanding the administration reverse these delays and get these toxic cleanup projects back on track – to protect military families and the communities living alongside these bases.
**[Now, we’re asking you to add your voice to ours. If you agree the Trump Administration must keep its promise and clean up these toxic chemicals by the original deadline, will you sign John’s petition?](https://act.johnfetterman.com/go/52386?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=1&akid=10464%2E434096%2EAXB47P)** 🙏 ✍️

clean_body: | | | BREAKING → Trump Administration DELAYING toxic chemical cleanups across the country Derek, we're not talking small delays either – in some cases, they're nearly a decade. Right here in PA, three bases are facing delays of up to five years. That’s unacceptable. 🤬 PFAS exposure is tied to cancers, weakened immune systems, birth defects – a whole list of serious health threats. And because PFAS-containing firefighting foam was used for decades on military sites, these chemicals have seeped straight into the drinking water of service members, their families, and surrounding communities. These families don’t have years to wait while Trump focuses on what he apparently considers more important – like starting trade wars or passing tax cuts for the ultrawealthy. 🙄 Their health is at risk right now. That’s why John joined 27 Senate colleagues in demanding the administration reverse these delays and get these toxic cleanup projects back on track – to protect military families and the communities living alongside these bases. 🙏 ✍️ | | Thanks, Derek. Team Fetterman 💛 | | | | Email us: info@johnfetterman.com

name: Haley Stevens
subject: this is an important moment for us
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Haley Stevens for Senate
body:
I'm not trying to be alarmist, but here's the truth

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. [To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here.](https://act.haleyformi.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?&t=1&akid=1572%2E49239%2EqkMY4H)
| | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10071?t=2&akid=1572%2E49239%2EqkMY4H)
---
Null, I just got out of an emergency meeting with my finance team, and I need to be honest with you:
**Fundraising has been brutally slow this week.** We’re pacing well behind where we need to be, and with GOP megadonors already flooding this race with dark money, we cannot afford to fall short.
That’s why we’ve set a $75,000 weekly fundraising goal. I know. It’s ambitious, but it's necessary. **[Will you rush $5 right now to help us hit this goal and keep Michigan blue?](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10072?t=3&akid=1572%2E49239%2EqkMY4H)**
| | [Donate](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10073?t=4&akid=1572%2E49239%2EqkMY4H)
---
I don’t mean to sound alarmist, but I do want to be clear: **This is one of the most important moments our campaign has faced so far.** MAGA Republicans are circling this seat. And if we fall behind now, I’m worried we won’t have the resources to fight back.
So I’m personally asking: **[Will you chip in $5 or whatever you can afford so we can hit this goal, stay on track, and win this race?](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10072?t=5&akid=1572%2E49239%2EqkMY4H)**
_Please use the links in this email to start a monthly donation through ActBlue._ | | | [Donate $5](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10072?t=6&akid=1572%2E49239%2EqkMY4H)
---
| | [Donate $25](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10074?t=7&akid=1572%2E49239%2EqkMY4H)
---
| | [Donate $50](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10075?t=8&akid=1572%2E49239%2EqkMY4H)
---
| | [Donate Another Amount](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10073?t=9&akid=1572%2E49239%2EqkMY4H)
---
Thank you for your urgent support,

Haley
|
* * *
**A note from Haley Stevens:** null, I’m proud to be a lifelong Michigander and the daughter of small-business owners. That’s why I’ve fought so hard for working families my entire life. Now, I’m running for Michigan’s open Senate seat to continue fighting for the people and communities I love. The path to the Senate majority runs right through Michigan, which means this race is shaping up to be the most competitive and expensive in the entire country. The GOP is already coming after me as a top target for defeat, so I’m counting on your grassroots support to fight back and win. [**If you’re in this fight with me, will you add your name to endorse my campaign?**](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10076?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=10&akid=1572%2E49239%2EqkMY4H) | | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10071?t=11&akid=1572%2E49239%2EqkMY4H)
---
You can also text “JOIN” to 80242 to become a core member of our team and receive exclusive, real-time updates from my campaign.
Thank you,
Haley

name: Deb Haaland
subject: Just $15
disclaimer_text: Paid for by State Democrats Committee (StateDemocrats
body:
That’s the donation I’m asking you to make before Dec. 15th
#
Just $15, Peter.
Deb Haaland here. That’s the donation I’m asking you to split between State Democrats Committee and my campaign before Dec. 15th so we can continue fighting for working families and build a stronger future for New Mexico.
**[Pitch in $15](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98450?refcode=em20251205-9805&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=15&t=1&akid=9805%2E614464%2EQFkWHJ)**

**$15** will help us reach our ambitious goal of raising $50,000 ahead of our mid-month deadline, helping us fight back against the flood of far-right attacks that are sure to come our way.
**$15** will help us launch TV and digital ads, ramp up our statewide organizing and continue traveling to every corner of the state to build undeniable support from New Mexicans everywhere.
**But we still need a lot of supporters to chip in $15 between now and our mid-month deadline, Peter.**

name: Team DNC
subject: On the importance of a strong Democratic Party:
body:
Midterm season is beginning, and your support is crucial.
---
Derek, 2025 is nearing its end, and as the year wraps up, we want to take a minute to reflect on the work we did together this year, and share what our path forward looks like ahead of next year’s midterms. Before we jump in, we’re asking you to [**make your first donation of the year to the DNC today.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-december-2025?refcode=em_20251205_5447_fr&amount=7&refcode2=5447_22930333_M4IMoS&akid=5447%2E22930333%2EM4IMoS) **Our work is powered by our team of generous grassroots supporters, and today, they’d love for you to join them.** Next year, we have an opportunity to flip Congress and put guardrails on Trump’s power — but to do it, we’ll need a massive number of resources. With the help of this grassroots team, we’ve already:
* Held town halls in every single state — red, blue, and everywhere in between — to connect with voters and discuss issues that actually matter to them.
* Invested in every state Democratic party to ensure that they have the resources to win key down-ballot races.
* **Began organizing everywhere so we can win anywhere.**

We’re already seeing that work pay off. Democrats swept elections across the country last month. But Derek, this is just the beginning. Heading into 2026, we’re committed to building on our success this year and continuing to organize, invest, and mobilize voters all over the country so we can deliver Trump and Republicans a major reckoning. We’re determined to flip the House and the Senate, and elect Democrats in state and down-ballot races who will check Trump’s power in their states, cities, and districts in the years to come. This is how we'll use that $7 contribution we're asking you to make today. [**If you’re ready to join us in our fight for the midterms next year, we’re ready to put your donation towards electing Democrats at all levels of government, in every corner of the country.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-december-2025?refcode=em_20251205_5447_fr&amount=7&refcode2=5447_22930333_M4IMoS&akid=5447%2E22930333%2EM4IMoS) _If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly:_ | | | **[CONTRIBUTE $7](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-december-2025?express_lane=true&amount=7&refcode=em_20251205_5447_fr&refcode2=5447_22930333_M4IMoS&akid=5447%2E22930333%2EM4IMoS)**
---
****
| **[CONTRIBUTE OTHER](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-december-2025?refcode=em_20251205_5447_fr&refcode2=5447_22930333_M4IMoS&akid=5447%2E22930333%2EM4IMoS)**
---
****
We’re so grateful to have you with us,
The Democrats

_If you gave recently via check or mail, our system may not have yet processed your gift. We are appreciative and thank you greatly for your support._

name: Tina Kotek
subject: I’ll always stand up for the Oregon we cherish
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Tina Kotek
body:
|
#
---
Peter, As Oregon’s Governor, I stand up for our values every single day. When President Trump tried to send the National Guard into Portland, I pushed back against his unlawful use of the military and kept troops off our streets. [_**I’m running for re-election because our work is far from finished, and the stakes are really high right now. Can you chip in any amount to help me fight to defend our shared values?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120710362/587145758/-1622755792?refcode=AU_TK_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20251205_PM2_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2M2MzZjUxZS0zZmQyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=6m6-iX4mZQAUcRXr3cb3eEynw2-XC_sYF9rYf_ybdGA=) | [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120710371/587145767/-1622755792?refcode=AU_TK_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20251205_PM2_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2M2MzZjUxZS0zZmQyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=6m6-iX4mZQAUcRXr3cb3eEynw2-XC_sYF9rYf_ybdGA=)

I fight to keep our state a place where everyone, regardless of their background, has a fair shot at success and can contribute authentically to their community.
I defend Oregon values by protecting abortion access and provider safety, supporting vote-by-mail, and leading the West Coast Health Alliance with other governors to safeguard public health.
But our hopes for Oregon are under threat. We are seeing the Trump administration attempt to dismantle the very foundations of our community and our democracy.
**I have, and always will, protect Oregon from these threats.**
From my start as an advocate at Oregon Food Bank to fighting to make sure every child has health care and a roof over their heads, I am driven by the North Star of building a stronger Oregon for _all of us._
As Governor, I'm pushing back against rising costs and am laser-focused on what matters:
* **Tackling the homelessness crisis** by declaring a State of Emergency, opening 1,700 new shelter beds, and keeping 24,000 households housed.
* **Easing financial burdens on families** by making prescription medication more affordable and protecting families from rising utility costs.
* **Fighting back against President Trump's damaging agenda** including his tariffs and cuts to Medicaid and food assistance programs.

I’m committed to bringing Oregonians together, beyond partisan lines, to create lasting change.
Whether it's investing in summer learning to better help our kids or expanding access to addiction treatment, _every step forward is a win for Oregon._
Together, we can keep Oregon a beacon of hope and progress for a country in turmoil. There’s more work to do, and I’m ready to lead us into a brighter future, protecting our values and our people from the challenges we face.

# [Trump Is Imploding With Young Voters--and Shuster Has the Receipts](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3722532&post_id=180845812&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgwODQ1ODEyLCJpYXQiOjE3NjQ5ODY2NTQsImV4cCI6MTc2NzU3ODY1NCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTM3MjI1MzIiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.EG4HfzZZtkYQ6B1Pq4N6l8gnyfkpnIojswKVnnTsoU0)
### A brutal poll crash, a Pentagon scandal, and the wildest political clip of the week. You need to see this breakdown.
| | [Blue Amp](https://substack.com/@blueamp) and [David Shuster](https://substack.com/@davidshuster)
---
| Dec 6
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3722532&post_id=180845812&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgwODQ1ODEyLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2NDk4NjY1NCwiZXhwIjoxNzY3NTc4NjU0LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMzcyMjUzMiIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.m415O6i3Z6z5pp-e9XxN5-_CgaiXFpKTAjukLfKs18k&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/blueamp/p/trump-is-imploding-with-young-votersand?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/965538b4-67cf-4011-9e92-331b13194810?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Our friend and compatriot, David Shuster, is back with a sharp, fast-moving breakdown of the political absurdities shaping 2026--and he wastes _zero_ time getting to the heart of it. From the very first beat, he shows how Donald Trump himself is now writing Democrats' ads...by talking. With that Gorgon-sized pucker-hole he calls a mouth.
Shuster walks viewers through [Trump's latest babbling assault on the concept of "affordability,"](https://substack.com/redirect/a9afdc6c-dd6d-4358-81c9-e28d1f6d48bc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) using it to illustrate a political reality the GOP simply does not want to talk about: **younger voters have turned on Trump with historic speed and intensity**. The numbers don't just trend against him--they look a lot like this car full of Nazis (how ironic!) from _Raiders of the Lost Ark_.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/965538b4-67cf-4011-9e92-331b13194810?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Shuster doesn't stop at the polling earthquake. He highlights an even more shocking shift under the surface: Americans under 35 increasingly see socialism _more favorably_ than capitalism, largely [because of the economic chaos and cruelty Trump has amplified](https://substack.com/redirect/8d665d5d-14c9-4d7b-9af3-55af3d4ed7bd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
As Shuster points out, Trump hasn't merely alienated young voters. He's reshaping the political landscape in ways the GOP can't control, barely understands, [and will pay a dire price for in the future](https://substack.com/redirect/d529add8-485f-4596-aff6-0c5593ab76a4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). There's an old about sleeping with the dogs that just may be apropos here.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/965538b4-67cf-4011-9e92-331b13194810?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Then he pivots to Trump's supposed "excellent health," which Shuster skewers with a perfect extra-dry-martini blend of sarcasm and incredulity. When Trumpworld releases glowing Trump-health language that sounds more beauty-pageant speech than doctor's report, Shuster knows just how to use that for comedic shrapnel.
Let's just say he has...notes.
* * *
Subscribe for free to get more David Shuster!
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.ET48cx5JQ0LhSoxJqthYvWbFeWYIOJGqGdUVVJuErPU?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=180845812)
* * *
And then the tone shifts. Shuster walks viewers into one of the most disturbing, underreported stories of the week. The Pentagon's deadly strike on a small vessel in the Caribbean, followed by a second shot that appears to have killed survivors who posed **no** imminent threat.
Lawmakers who viewed the unreleased footage describe it as one of the most troubling acts by our government they've ever seen. Shuster uses their testimony to raise serious questions about [transparency, accountability, and war crimes](https://substack.com/redirect/f442fe8b-b3ac-485b-9f52-17b11a12e073?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--questions the public desperately needs to hear.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/965538b4-67cf-4011-9e92-331b13194810?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
He also spotlights a disturbing civil-rights incident in Key Largo, where a woman in medical scrubs was detained simply for asserting her constitutional rights. Another example of how Trump's police state government has spread under his Administration.
Finally, because it's Shuster, the episode ends with a laugh you absolutely won't see coming--an AI musical moment that takes a bizarre turn and somehow still makes perfect sense in the world in which we're living.
**Watch the full video. It 's sharp, funny, urgent, and absolutely worth your time.**
And at the rate things are going, don't be surprised when FIFA announces a _World Peace Prize_ ...and immediately awards it to Donald Trump for "almost not starting a war this week!"
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/965538b4-67cf-4011-9e92-331b13194810?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|

From the very first beat, he shows how Donald Trump himself is now writing Democrats' ads...by talking. With that Gorgon-sized pucker-hole he calls a mouth. The numbers don't just trend against him--they look a lot like this car full of Nazis (how ironic!) from Raiders of the Lost Ark. | | ---|---|--- Shuster doesn't stop at the polling earthquake. name: Brian Lenney
subject: How to Get Played by Meta While Thinking You’re Protecting Kids
body:
Dismantling Congressman John James's App Store Accountability Act (a Masterclass in Corporate Manipulation)

| |
---|---|---
| | | Forwarded this email? [Subscribe here](https://substack..BolAyQxigc7uk5rmVKhTavXMUScNb4xptHW4OIXfQsI?) for more
---
# [How to Get Played by Meta While Thinking You're Protecting Kids](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2114823&post_id=180848795&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgwODQ4Nzk1LCJpYXQiOjE3NjQ5ODMxMzMsImV4cCI6MTc2NzU3NTEzMywiaXNzIjoicHViLTIxMTQ4MjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.5LFWFTtoTH5sOkegTRKQBre5B-75E3tU1B60EXTvE2M)
### Dismantling Congressman John James's App Store Accountability Act (a Masterclass in Corporate Manipulation)
| | [Brian Lenney](https://substack.com/@brianlenney)
---
| Dec 6
---
| [](https://substack.com/@brianlenney)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2114823&post_id=180848795&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgwODQ4Nzk1LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2NDk4MzEzMywiZXhwIjoxNzY3NTc1MTMzLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMjExNDgyMyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.wljBuqFpFMqolkGTG-hbILFOO5VCO1o8otsBelwA0pc&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2114823&post_id=180848795&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgwODQ4Nzk1LCJpYXQiOjE3NjQ5ODMxMzMsImV4cCI6MTc2NzU3NTEzMywiaXNzIjoicHViLTIxMTQ4MjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.5LFWFTtoTH5sOkegTRKQBre5B-75E3tU1B60EXTvE2M&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2114823&post_id=180848795&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgwODQ4Nzk1LCJpYXQiOjE3NjQ5ODMxMzMsImV4cCI6MTc2NzU3NTEzMywiaXNzIjoicHViLTIxMTQ4MjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.5LFWFTtoTH5sOkegTRKQBre5B-75E3tU1B60EXTvE2M)
---
| | [](https://substack..c7do1DcgrlWTbwjmwSBUmzFdH4yNF2COX1Zq_J4COJU?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/brianlenney/p/how-to-get-played-by-meta-while-thinking?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

I just watched [this video](https://substack.com/redirect/32b88b06-946e-40fb-88a2-f212533e1553?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of Congressman John James pushing the "App Store Accountability Act." Every argument he (and his witness) make has a fatal flaw.
Check it out...
[](https://substack.com/redirect/80dc957f-faa9-4880-93fb-b9ab11ad5573?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
So now, I'm going to expose each one...
* * *
## **CLAIM #1:** "I've said for years, Facebook is the Philip Morris of our time, yet they are just one example of the cesspool that is the modern internet."
You're absolutely right, Congressman.
Facebook IS the Philip Morris of our time. They knowingly poison children for profit. Their internal research proved it.
So here's my question:
_**When Philip Morris was poisoning Americans, did Congress pass a law regulating convenience stores?**_
No... they regulated PHILIP MORRIS.
They made the tobacco companies put warning labels on THEIR products. They banned THEM from marketing to children. They held THEM accountable for the harm THEY created.
But your bill doesn't do that.
Your bill regulates Apple and Google (i.e. the convenience stores). While Facebook keeps serving algorithmic poison directly to children's brains, Apple gets stuck with compliance paperwork.
If Facebook is Philip Morris, why are you regulating 7-Eleven?
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/837f4a0d-c2b2-4e47-a962-a3af7039a701?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* * *
## **CLAIM #2: "**A national poll commissioned by Digital Childhood Alliance found that 88% of parents want app stores to require parental approval before minors can download a new app. 88%. Can you imagine any other issue that 88% of Americans agree on?"
Congressman, let me tell you about the organization that commissioned that poll you just cited.
The Digital Childhood Alliance is the same group whose executive director, Casey Stefanski, sat before the Louisiana Senate Finance Committee and refused to disclose which tech companies fund her organization.
Senator Jay Morris asked her directly: "Are you funded by tech companies?"
She claimed she "didn't feel comfortable" answering. When pressed for a yes-or-no answer, she finally admitted they receive tech company funding but refused to name the companies.
_**Bloomberg later reported that Meta (Facebook 's parent company) was bankrolling this "grassroots" movement.**_
So when you cite that 88% statistic... [you're citing a poll commissioned by a Meta-funded astroturf organization designed to push legislation that protects Meta from liability](https://substack.com/redirect/6d6c1086-6fa2-4253-9de4-2865c3842dc9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
You're quoting Facebook's propaganda as if it's independent research. The question isn't whether parents want control (of course they do). The question is: why does Meta want you to regulate Apple instead of them?
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/bd4728e4-cd68-4cf6-85d2-6ce9a46f3550?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* * *
## **CLAIM #3: "**Just as brick and mortar stores are held responsible for selling age restricted materials like tobacco or alcohol to minors, the App Store Accountability Act will hold digital app stores accountable for providing adult or addictive material to minors as well."
This analogy is completely backwards, and I can prove it.
When we regulate alcohol sales to minors, we do TWO things:
1. We require the STORE to verify age at point of sale
2. We require the MANUFACTURER to ensure their product isn't marketed to children

_**Your bill only does #1**_
Idaho, Utah, and others got this right when they passed pornography age verification laws. We didn't regulate internet service providers. We didn't regulate app stores. We didn't regulate payment processors.
We went straight to the source: the pornography sites themselves.
Pornhub had to verify ages BEFORE serving explicit content. The law hit so hard that Pornhub geoblocked Idaho entirely rather than comply.
That's what real accountability looks like. Now let's apply that same logic to social media:
* Alcohol stores verify age -> App stores verify age.
* Beer companies can't market to kids -> Social media companies CAN still serve harmful algorithmic content to children.

Your bill creates the illusion of protection while letting Instagram's algorithm keep pushing eating disorder content to 13-year-old girls. The tobacco store has to check ID, but Philip Morris can still advertise cigarettes in teen magazines.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/cf34eb04-dba8-40ca-905d-f81e57b707b1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* * *
## **CLAIM #4: "**Kids cannot consent. I say again, kids cannot consent, and any company that exposes them to adult or addictive material should and will be held accountable."
I agree 100%.
_Kids cannot consent._
_**So why does your bill let the companies serving the harmful content off the hook?**_
Meta's own internal research ([exposed through Frances Haugen's whistleblower documents](https://substack.com/redirect/033a1189-67bc-4538-ac6f-4055b813a621?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)) showed that Instagram made body image issues worse for one in three teen girls. They documented how their algorithms pushed vulnerable users toward content about eating disorders and suicide.
Instagram created the harmful content. Instagram designed the addictive algorithms. Instagram serves the material that's destroying children's mental health.
_**But your bill makes Apple verify ages while Instagram faces zero new restrictions on what they serve AFTER the download.**_
Here's the problem:
You say companies that expose kids to harmful material should be held accountable but Meta exposes kids to harmful material (proven by their own research), and what do you do? The bill you were handed pins it on Apple (not Meta).
That's not accountability.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/01b68755-7cf3-4a5c-aa88-75e895c0b75e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* * *
## **CLAIM #5: "**App stores are a gateway to the internet for social media, for our children."
Now this... this reveals the entire flaw in your approach.
You're treating app stores like a GATE when they're actually just a DOOR.
Once a child walks through that door (downloads Instagram, Facebook, et al), your bill does NOTHING to protect them from what happens inside the house (algorithmic content).
Even worse: Meta can serve harmful content through web browsers without touching your app store "gate" at all.
A 13-year-old can access Instagram through Safari or Chrome, use Instagram on a friend's phone, or access social media apps through any of the thousand workarounds teenagers perfected years ago.
Your "gate" has a hundred unlocked back doors.
_**Meanwhile, Idaho 's pornography law actually works because it regulates the content source, not the distribution method. **_
Pornhub can't serve explicit content to minors regardless of whether they access it through an app, web browser, friend's phone, or any other method. We locked the HOUSE (i.e. age verification), not the DOOR (app store bills).
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/baeb2c41-f82b-4788-9e9c-8c7db3cc70b6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* * *
## **CLAIM #6: "**They have the data already. They already have all of the access to this information. And not only that, they already are developing the tools currently."
You know who ELSE already has the data and tools to verify ages...?
_**FACEBOOK AND INSTAGRAM.**_
When someone creates an Instagram account, they enter their birthdate. Meta collects massive amounts of personal data: location, interests, relationships, psychological vulnerabilities. They use this to serve targeted content designed to maximize engagement.
But somehow we should worry about Apple knowing a birthdate? Here's what your "witness" isn't telling you:
_**Meta WANTS app stores handling verification because it shifts all liability away from Meta.**_
If Apple verifies the age and approves the download, Meta can say: "Hey, the parent approved it. Not our problem what content our algorithm serves after that." Meta creates the harm. Apple handles the paperwork. Meta faces zero accountability.
_**This is exactly what Meta paid the Digital Childhood Alliance to accomplish.**_
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/9a4fa4e1-2325-4ff0-ac26-2e577288ae2b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* * *
## **CLAIM #7: "**They're the ones calling themselves a store, not us. So if they want to be a store, we should hold them at the same accountability as any other store in any other market."
Fine...
_Let 's actually apply store accountability consistently:_
When a convenience store sells alcohol to a minor:
* The STORE gets fined for not checking ID
* The ALCOHOL MANUFACTURER also faces consequences for their product's harm

When a pharmacy dispenses medication improperly:
* The PHARMACY gets sanctioned
* The DRUG MANUFACTURER is also held accountable for product safety

_**Your bill only targets the first part.**_
Apple verifies ages (like the convenience store checking ID). But Instagram faces no new requirements to ensure their "product" (algorithmic content) isn't harmful to children. Real store accountability means regulating BOTH the distributor AND the manufacturer.
Your bill only regulates the distributor while giving the manufacturer a liability shield.
Weird.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/58cd847d-04d2-4669-abbe-00c3c9a29d12?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* * *
## **CLAIM #8: "**Most importantly, they know the age of their user better than any other company in the world."
This is the most dishonest claim in the entire testimony.
Because Meta knows users' ages better than anyone.
They collect it during account creation. They track behavior to infer age even when users lie. They target ads based on precise age demographics. But your witness wants you to believe Apple knows age better than Meta?
Instagram requires users to enter birthdates. Facebook has decade-long user histories. Meta's advertising platform lets you target "13-year-old girls interested in fitness" with surgical precision.
_**They know EXACTLY how old their users are.**_
In fact, Meta's own documents ([exposed in lawsuits](https://substack.com/redirect/2bc79df8-b589-4d89-814a-b7131a6a310d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)) show they deliberately targeted children as young as 13 with "school blast" notifications designed to embed Instagram into school communities.
They knew the ages. They knew the schools. They knew exactly what they were doing.
_So why does your bill let them pretend they don 't know?_
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/df0a3224-3cd2-42ef-a217-5433c8a5406b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* * *
## **CLAIM #9: "**Apple itself has said that they can actually do all of this in a privacy conscious way."
Apple CAN verify ages in a privacy-conscious way.
But that's not the question.
The question is:_why should Apple bear responsibility for Meta 's harmful content?_
You're forcing Apple to build expensive verification infrastructure so Meta can keep serving algorithmic poison to kids with a liability shield. Let me give you the alternative:
Make Meta verify ages BEFORE serving content, just like Idaho made Pornhub verify ages before serving explicit material.
Then, Meta bears the compliance cost, faces liability for age verification failures, can't hide behind "but the parent approved the download," and best of all... _**parents maintain actual control because the platform serving harmful content is now accountable.**_
But your bill protects Meta's profits while burdening their competitors.
That's not an accident.
That's exactly what Meta paid for when _they funded_ the Digital Childhood Alliance.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/3b282c46-863c-46bf-8369-7c67e6012c97?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* * *
## **THE PATTERN YOU 'RE MISSING, CONGRESSMAN**
Every argument you made has the same fatal flaw:
**You correctly identify Meta/Facebook as the harm source, then propose legislation that regulates everyone EXCEPT Meta.**
Now, read that again.
It's like watching someone say:
"This arsonist keeps burning down houses"
"We need accountability"
"So I'm passing a law requiring fire departments to inspect matches"
_**All while the arsonist keeps lighting fires.**_
You cited 83 letters from "parents and groups" supporting your bill.
Did you check which of those groups receive Meta funding? Did you verify that the Digital Childhood Alliance poll wasn't designed by Meta's lobbying team? Did you ask why Tim Cook personally called governors to oppose these measures while Meta deployed 12 lobbyists in Louisiana alone?
Did you wonder why Meta spent $24 million on lobbying in 2024 (more than Lockheed Martin and Boeing) while publicly claiming they aren't the "driving force" behind your legislation?
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/d7ad0652-27c0-4646-a90f-755669775204?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* * *
## **WHAT REAL ACCOUNTABILITY LOOKS LIKE**
If you actually want to protect children from social media harm:
Make Meta, TikTok, and Instagram verify ages BEFORE serving content. Place liability on platforms for harmful algorithmic content served to verified minors. Require platforms to disclose what content their algorithms promote to children.
Idaho's pornography law works because it regulates content creators, not distributors. Pornhub had to comply or geoblock the entire state.
_**That 's real accountability.**_
Thing is, parents already have the power to:
✅ Refuse to give children smartphones...
✅ Deny social media access...
✅ Monitor device usage...
✅ Set boundaries and enforce them...
Your bill doesn't "empower parents."
It creates government-mandated paperwork that parents will click through without reading (just like they do with Terms of Service). When Meta backs legislation that burdens its competitors, that's corporate warfare (not child protection). And when 88% polling comes from Meta-funded groups, that's what we call... _propaganda._
Congressman, you opened by calling Facebook "the Philip Morris of our time."
_You were right._
But then you proposed legislation that regulates convenience stores while Philip Morris keeps selling cigarettes to children.
_**Meta is playing you. The Digital Childhood Alliance is playing you. And while you hold hearings about "accountability," Meta's algorithm is destroying another generation of teenage girls.**_
Want to actually protect kids?
Make the companies creating the harm fix the problem they created.
Not the app stores. Not the payment processors. Not the internet service providers. The platforms serving algorithmic poison to children should bear the burden of protecting children from that poison.
Anything less isn't accountability.
But it IS exactly what Meta paid for.
* * *
_Would the Congressman like to reconsider whose interests his bill actually serves?_

clean_body: Dismantling Congressman John James's App Store Accountability Act (a Masterclass in Corporate Manipulation) | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more Dismantling Congressman John James's App Store Accountability Act (a Masterclass in Corporate Manipulation) | | | Dec 6 | | | | | | | | | | | | I just watched of Congressman John James pushing the "App Store Accountability Act." Every argument he (and his witness) make has a fatal flaw. Check it out... So now, I'm going to expose each one... CLAIM #1: "I've said for years, Facebook is the Philip Morris of our time, yet they are just one example of the cesspool that is the modern internet." You're absolutely right, Congressman. Facebook IS the Philip Morris of our time. They knowingly poison children for profit. Their internal research proved it. So here's my question: When Philip Morris was poisoning Americans, did Congress pass a law regulating convenience stores? No... they regulated PHILIP MORRIS. They made the tobacco companies put warning labels on THEIR products. They banned THEM from marketing to children. They held THEM accountable for the harm THEY created. But your bill doesn't do that. Your bill regulates Apple and Google (i.e. the convenience stores). While Facebook keeps serving algorithmic poison directly to children's brains, Apple gets stuck with compliance paperwork. If Facebook is Philip Morris, why are you regulating 7-Eleven? | | ---|---|--- CLAIM #2: "A national poll commissioned by Digital Childhood Alliance found that 88% of parents want app stores to require parental approval before minors can download a new app. 88%. Can you imagine any other issue that 88% of Americans agree on?" Congressman, let me tell you about the organization that commissioned that poll you just cited. The Digital Childhood Alliance is the same group whose executive director, Casey Stefanski, sat before the Louisiana Senate Finance Committee and refused to disclose which tech companies fund her organization. Senator Jay Morris asked her directly: "Are you funded by tech companies?" She claimed she "didn't feel comfortable" answering. When pressed for a yes-or-no answer, she finally admitted they receive tech company funding but refused to name the companies. Bloomberg later reported that Meta (Facebook 's parent company) was bankrolling this "grassroots" movement. So when you cite that 88% statistic... . You're quoting Facebook's propaganda as if it's independent research. The question isn't whether parents want control (of course they do). The question is: why does Meta want you to regulate Apple instead of them? | | ---|---|--- CLAIM #3: "Just as brick and mortar stores are held responsible for selling age restricted materials like tobacco or alcohol to minors, the App Store Accountability Act will hold digital app stores accountable for providing adult or addictive material to minors as well." This analogy is completely backwards, and I can prove it. When we regulate alcohol sales to minors, we do TWO things: 1. We require the STORE to verify age at point of sale 2. We require the MANUFACTURER to ensure their product isn't marketed to children Your bill only does #1 Idaho, Utah, and others got this right when they passed pornography age verification laws. We didn't regulate internet service providers. We didn't regulate app stores. We didn't regulate payment processors. We went straight to the source: the pornography sites themselves. Pornhub had to verify ages BEFORE serving explicit content. The law hit so hard that Pornhub geoblocked Idaho entirely rather than comply. That's what real accountability looks like. Now let's apply that same logic to social media: Alcohol stores verify age -> App stores verify age. Beer companies can't market to kids -> Social media companies CAN still serve harmful algorithmic content to children. Your bill creates the illusion of protection while letting Instagram's algorithm keep pushing eating disorder content to 13-year-old girls. The tobacco store has to check ID, but Philip Morris can still advertise cigarettes in teen magazines. | | ---|---|--- CLAIM #4: "Kids cannot consent. I say again, kids cannot consent, and any company that exposes them to adult or addictive material should and will be held accountable." I agree 100%. Kids cannot consent. So why does your bill let the companies serving the harmful content off the hook? Meta's own internal research () showed that Instagram made body image issues worse for one in three teen girls. They documented how their algorithms pushed vulnerable users toward content about eating disorders and suicide. Instagram created the harmful content. Instagram designed the addictive algorithms. Instagram serves the material that's destroying children's mental health. But your bill makes Apple verify ages while Instagram faces zero new restrictions on what they serve AFTER the download. Here's the problem: You say companies that expose kids to harmful material should be held accountable but Meta exposes kids to harmful material (proven by their own research), and what do you do? The bill you were handed pins it on Apple (not Meta). That's not accountability. | | ---|---|--- CLAIM #5: "App stores are a gateway to the internet for social media, for our children." Now this... this reveals the entire flaw in your approach. You're treating app stores like a GATE when they're actually just a DOOR. Once a child walks through that door (downloads Instagram, Facebook, et al), your bill does NOTHING to protect them from what happens inside the house (algorithmic content). Even worse: Meta can serve harmful content through web browsers without touching your app store "gate" at all. A 13-year-old can access Instagram through Safari or Chrome, use Instagram on a friend's phone, or access social media apps through any of the thousand workarounds teenagers perfected years ago. Your "gate" has a hundred unlocked back doors. Meanwhile, Idaho 's pornography law actually works because it regulates the content source, not the distribution method. Pornhub can't serve explicit content to minors regardless of whether they access it through an app, web browser, friend's phone, or any other method. We locked the HOUSE (i.e. age verification), not the DOOR (app store bills). | | ---|---|--- CLAIM #6: "They have the data already. They already have all of the access to this information. And not only that, they already are developing the tools currently." You know who ELSE already has the data and tools to verify ages...? FACEBOOK AND INSTAGRAM. When someone creates an Instagram account, they enter their birthdate. Meta collects massive amounts of personal data: location, interests, relationships, psychological vulnerabilities. They use this to serve targeted content designed to maximize engagement. But somehow we should worry about Apple knowing a birthdate? Here's what your "witness" isn't telling you: Meta WANTS app stores handling verification because it shifts all liability away from Meta. If Apple verifies the age and approves the download, Meta can say: "Hey, the parent approved it. Not our problem what content our algorithm serves after that." Meta creates the harm. Apple handles the paperwork. Meta faces zero accountability. This is exactly what Meta paid the Digital Childhood Alliance to accomplish. | | ---|---|--- CLAIM #7: "They're the ones calling themselves a store, not us. So if they want to be a store, we should hold them at the same accountability as any other store in any other market." Fine... Let 's actually apply store accountability consistently: When a convenience store sells alcohol to a minor: The STORE gets fined for not checking ID The ALCOHOL MANUFACTURER also faces consequences for their product's harm When a pharmacy dispenses medication improperly: The PHARMACY gets sanctioned The DRUG MANUFACTURER is also held accountable for product safety Your bill only targets the first part. Apple verifies ages (like the convenience store checking ID). But Instagram faces no new requirements to ensure their "product" (algorithmic content) isn't harmful to children. Real store accountability means regulating BOTH the distributor AND the manufacturer. Your bill only regulates the distributor while giving the manufacturer a liability shield. Weird. | | ---|---|--- CLAIM #8: "Most importantly, they know the age of their user better than any other company in the world." This is the most dishonest claim in the entire testimony. Because Meta knows users' ages better than anyone. They collect it during account creation. They track behavior to infer age even when users lie. They target ads based on precise age demographics. But your witness wants you to believe Apple knows age better than Meta? Instagram requires users to enter birthdates. Facebook has decade-long user histories. Meta's advertising platform lets you target "13-year-old girls interested in fitness" with surgical precision. They know EXACTLY how old their users are. In fact, Meta's own documents () show they deliberately targeted children as young as 13 with "school blast" notifications designed to embed Instagram into school communities. They knew the ages. They knew the schools. They knew exactly what they were doing. So why does your bill let them pretend they don 't know? | | ---|---|--- CLAIM #9: "Apple itself has said that they can actually do all of this in a privacy conscious way." Apple CAN verify ages in a privacy-conscious way. But that's not the question. The question is:why should Apple bear responsibility for Meta 's harmful content? You're forcing Apple to build expensive verification infrastructure so Meta can keep serving algorithmic poison to kids with a liability shield. Let me give you the alternative: Make Meta verify ages BEFORE serving content, just like Idaho made Pornhub verify ages before serving explicit material. Then, Meta bears the compliance cost, faces liability for age verification failures, can't hide behind "but the parent approved the download," and best of all... parents maintain actual control because the platform serving harmful content is now accountable. But your bill protects Meta's profits while burdening their competitors. That's not an accident. That's exactly what Meta paid for when they funded the Digital Childhood Alliance. | | ---|---|--- THE PATTERN YOU 'RE MISSING, CONGRESSMAN Every argument you made has the same fatal flaw: You correctly identify Meta/Facebook as the harm source, then propose legislation that regulates everyone EXCEPT Meta. Now, read that again. It's like watching someone say: "This arsonist keeps burning down houses" "We need accountability" "So I'm passing a law requiring fire departments to inspect matches" All while the arsonist keeps lighting fires. You cited 83 letters from "parents and groups" supporting your bill. Did you check which of those groups receive Meta funding? Did you verify that the Digital Childhood Alliance poll wasn't designed by Meta's lobbying team? Did you ask why Tim Cook personally called governors to oppose these measures while Meta deployed 12 lobbyists in Louisiana alone? Did you wonder why Meta spent $24 million on lobbying in 2024 (more than Lockheed Martin and Boeing) while publicly claiming they aren't the "driving force" behind your legislation? | | ---|---|--- WHAT REAL ACCOUNTABILITY LOOKS LIKE If you actually want to protect children from social media harm: Make Meta, TikTok, and Instagram verify ages BEFORE serving content. Place liability on platforms for harmful algorithmic content served to verified minors. Require platforms to disclose what content their algorithms promote to children. Idaho's pornography law works because it regulates content creators, not distributors. Pornhub had to comply or geoblock the entire state. That 's real accountability. Thing is, parents already have the power to: ✅ Refuse to give children smartphones... ✅ Deny social media access... ✅ Monitor device usage... ✅ Set boundaries and enforce them... Your bill doesn't "empower parents." It creates government-mandated paperwork that parents will click through without reading (just like they do with Terms of Service). When Meta backs legislation that burdens its competitors, that's corporate warfare (not child protection). And when 88% polling comes from Meta-funded groups, that's what we call... propaganda. Congressman, you opened by calling Facebook "the Philip Morris of our time." You were right. But then you proposed legislation that regulates convenience stores while Philip Morris keeps selling cigarettes to children. Meta is playing you. The Digital Childhood Alliance is playing you. And while you hold hearings about "accountability," Meta's algorithm is destroying another generation of teenage girls. Want to actually protect kids? Make the companies creating the harm fix the problem they created. Not the app stores. Not the payment processors. Not the internet service providers. The platforms serving algorithmic poison to children should bear the burden of protecting children from that poison. Anything less isn't accountability. But it IS exactly what Meta paid for. Would the Congressman like to reconsider whose interests his bill actually serves? | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Brian Lenney ? ?

Hey folks,
**Joe Walsh** and I just went live for a _Welcome to the Party, Pal_ that cuts right to the bone. From the DC pipe bomber to corrupt pardons to the GOP's total collapse, this one moves fast and hits hard. You'll feel the urgency from the first minute.
* **[00:01:12]** Joe's six months as a Democrat and why GOP "surprise" at RFK Jr. is total nonsense.
* **[00:05:58]** The DC pipe bomber found, Bongino admits he got paid to spread lies, and we dig into right-wing media incentives.
* **[00:14:06]** Trump's corrupt pardons vs. Biden's, and what unequal justice does to public trust.
* **[00:22:24]** The alternate timeline where Biden really was "the bridge" and how 2024 might've looked.
* **[00:29:41]** Pete Hegseth, "narco-terrorists," and the dangerous expansion of post-9/11 executive power.

This episode goes deep on how money, media, and power distort our politics--and how corruption on both sides leaves people doubting the rule of law. Joe and I stay honest about what's at stake and what it'll take to keep this country from sleepwalking into something darker. If you value conversations like this, help me keep them going.
**[Become a paid subscriber to Endless Urgency today»](https://substack.com/redirect/ec63fc78-3ec1-42ad-8d8f-4af833082bac?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
With urgency,
--Mike

name: Eric Swalwell via VPP
subject: It’s official: I’m running for Governor
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
I’m excited to share the news!
| [](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/98104?t=6&akid=21993%2E878179%2EHHNNwP) Hi friend I made a huge announcement on Jimmy Kimmel: **_I’m running for Governor of California._**

I chose to make this announcement with Jimmy Kimmel for a reason. If Trump thinks he’s going to silence either of us, he’s got another thing coming.

[I’m counting on your support to win this race. Will you split a donation between me and the Voter Protection Project to help me build a winning campaign from the very start?](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/98104?t=7&akid=21993%2E878179%2EHHNNwP) [](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/98104?t=8&akid=21993%2E878179%2EHHNNwP) | [DONATE NOW »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/98105?t=9&akid=21993%2E878179%2EHHNNwP)
---
I’m running for Governor because I believe in the California promise: work hard and dream bigger for your kids. That’s exactly what I’ve done as a husband, father of three, and one of the most outspoken members of Congress.

**The next Governor needs to be laser-focused on one thing: making California affordable. That’s what this campaign is all about and why I’m running.**

It’s time to build a new California where families can afford to get ahead, where small businesses have the opportunity to grow, and where we modernize our economy to work for everyone.

[If that sounds good, I need your help. Our goal is to raise $1 million in the first days of this campaign, but I can’t do it without you. Will you split a donation now between my campaign and the Voter Protection Project to kick this campaign off the right way?](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/98104?t=10&akid=21993%2E878179%2EHHNNwP)
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation between Eric Swalwell and VPP will go through immediately:
[CONTRIBUTE $10 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/98106?t=11&akid=21993%2E878179%2EHHNNwP)
---
[CONTRIBUTE $25 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/98107?t=12&akid=21993%2E878179%2EHHNNwP)

[Or a custom donation »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/98110?t=15&akid=21993%2E878179%2EHHNNwP)
Thanks for your support!
Congressman Eric Swalwell
| | Paid for by VPP
(www.protectvoting.org)
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=21993%2E878179%2EHHNNwP). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=21993%2E878179%2EHHNNwP).**

[Click here](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=21993%2E878179%2EHHNNwP) to request fewer emails. clean_body: I’m excited to share the news! | Hi friend I made a huge announcement on Jimmy Kimmel: I’m running for Governor of California. I chose to make this announcement with Jimmy Kimmel for a reason. If Trump thinks he’s going to silence either of us, he’s got another thing coming. | I’m running for Governor because I believe in the California promise: work hard and dream bigger for your kids. That’s exactly what I’ve done as a husband, father of three, and one of the most outspoken members of Congress. The next Governor needs to be laser-focused on one thing: making California affordable. That’s what this campaign is all about and why I’m running. It’s time to build a new California where families can afford to get ahead, where small businesses have the opportunity to grow, and where we modernize our economy to work for everyone. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation between Eric Swalwell and VPP will go through immediately: Thanks for your support! Congressman Eric Swalwell | | Paid for by VPP (www.protectvoting.org) Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104 The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps. Please, consider making a . Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a. to request fewer emails. Click here to . | |

body:
| | | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/14f5447b085a/delightful-december?e=8249791cac)
---
| | | | [|
---](https://briannaforcolorado.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e7333941d4549e2ed73aa08fb&id=d49788a207&e=8249791cac)
| | | | | | | | | | | | Happy (finally) winter everybody! It took way too long to get snow, so I am glad it is finally here.
This month’s edition will discuss upcoming constituent and [community](https://briannaforcolorado.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e7333941d4549e2ed73aa08fb&id=1793204065&e=8249791cac) events, and in case you missed it headlines. But first, let’s talk about some stuff I have been up to!
---
|
---
| | | | | | | | | | |
---
| | | On November 18th, my aide Izzy and I attended [Feeding Colorado’s ](https://briannaforcolorado.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e7333941d4549e2ed73aa08fb&id=b7d0e4ad9b&e=8249791cac)Legislative Day of Action at the [Food Bank of the Rockies](https://briannaforcolorado.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e7333941d4549e2ed73aa08fb&id=f4034b916d&e=8249791cac). Between the legislators and their aides, we were able to pack 2,075 pounds of food which equates to 2,563 meals!
---
| | | | | | | | | | |
---
| |
---
| | On November 19th, I attended [Electing Women’s](https://briannaforcolorado.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e7333941d4549e2ed73aa08fb&id=8c9c2f3b51&e=8249791cac) fundraiser for [Gov. Janet Mills](https://briannaforcolorado.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e7333941d4549e2ed73aa08fb&id=ab916a5164&e=8249791cac) of Maine.
---
| | | | | | | | | | |
---
| |
---
| | On November 20th, I went on 9News with Anaya Robinson and Maya Blasingame to talk about Transgender Day of Remembrance. You can find our interview [here](https://briannaforcolorado.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e7333941d4549e2ed73aa08fb&id=cc09585376&e=8249791cac).
---
|
---

---
| | | | | | | | | | |
---
|
---
| | | **December 6th, 10 am, 911 10th St., Golden**
Swing by City Hall at 10:00 a.m. for this month’s Coffee with Councilors, where things are getting a little festive (and competitive!) with a friendly Councilor bakeoff.
Councilors will bring their best baked goods for an end-of-year bake-off. You get to taste and vote!
City staff will also be chatting about supporting Golden’s local businesses during the holiday season. Afterward, stroll around Golden and keep that shopping momentum going!
---
| | **GET YOUR PICTURES WITH SANTA**
---
| | | | | | | | | | |
---
|
---
| | | **December 9th, 6:30-8pm, 1470 10th St., Golden**
Calling all children: get your picture with Santa! Then stick around for free cookies, coloring, crafts, and more!
The City will provide free light refreshments and activities for kids. Remember to bring your letters to Santa. Elves from the North Pole along with our very own Golden Police Officers will be on hand to collect your letters to and make sure they get to the Big Man himself.
---

---
|
---

name: MGP
subject: Looking ahead together
body:
| | One more note from me today, Peter –

We know exactly the difference that early grassroots support makes in a competitive race like this. Whether or not we can overcome the challenges ahead depends on what we build right now.

When I think about the next year – the fights we’re going to take on, the families counting on us, the national pressure already coming down on this district – I know we have to hit the ground running.

[That starts with showing early grassroots strength. Can you pitch in $10 before midnight?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120711007/587156957/-1021815558?amount=10&refcode=MGP_EM_FR_180DO_251205_2_2&refcode2=9d0fb350-4cd2-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTkyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5ZDBmYjM1MC00Y2QyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=TSbw8AXKIXEG_stIWlqkX22MAEzwMLiuzQBRlkJv7C4=) _If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately._ | **[EXPRESS DONATE: $10](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120711008/587156958/-1021815558?amount=10&express_lane=true&refcode=MGP_EM_FR_180DO_251205_2_2&refcode2=9d0fb350-4cd2-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTkyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5ZDBmYjM1MC00Y2QyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=TSbw8AXKIXEG_stIWlqkX22MAEzwMLiuzQBRlkJv7C4=)**
---

We’ve never been the campaign with the biggest wallets or flashiest donors. Our strength has always come from regular people who care deeply about this community and want a representative who actually listens.

That’s what got us here, and it’s how we’ll protect this seat in 2026.

Thanks for having my back as always, Peter

– Marie

|
