clean_body: Map manipulators are attempting to silence the voices of communities of color to hold onto their power at all costs. | Peter, As you read this, map manipulators who have thrown the country into a redistricting crisis are now setting their sights on South Carolina. They’re hoping to force through a gerrymandered map that would help Republicans gain an extra seat in Congress. And the strategy they’re deploying is not subtle. They’re going after Rep. Jim Clyburn’s district — which is 46% Black — which could result in a map where voters of color have less of a say in who gets to represent their interests in Washington. We must call this what it is: A blatant attempt to silence voters of color and sideline Black leaders in the halls of power. All On The Line is preparing for fights like these in states across the country, which we expect to shift into high gear in the weeks ahead.= If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = = | = = | = These cynical strategies have been used by map manipulators for years in an attempt to drown out the voices of voters of color. In particular, politicians will try to “crack and pack” majority-minority districts: splitting Black and Brown communities among multiple districts, making it harder for these communities to select representatives of their choosing. Take Missouri, for example — another state where map manipulators forced through a mid-decade gerrymander that carved up Kansas City into three separate districts. This new map used the same street, Troost Avenue, that was used to segregate white and Black communities during Jim Crow. It’s not hyperbole to say that this gerrymandering fight is our generation’s test of whether we will stand up and defend civil rights in America. And that’s exactly what All On The Line is doing in states across the country, wherever anti-democracy politicians are trying to silence communities of color. = Thank you for stepping up in this watershed moment for our democracy. — Team All On The Line is the grassroots advocacy campaign supported by the National Redistricting Action Fund. Support our work to end gerrymandering. Text FAIR to 88228 to receive recurring updates from AOTL by automated text message. Text HELP for help, STOP to end. body:
but it’s actually pretty important for Colorado

[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120760594/587279587/-1473932214?refcode=logo&amount=10&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83MjY1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZjY4OTFkOS1kZWQyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=l84J8aSgx7u77GFtR70PVuwMEsgy3XepEDS4iIulmIU=) Hi team, Jacob here. I’m the Digital Director for John Hickenlooper’s campaign. My job is a lot of what goes on behind the curtain: sending texts and emails like this one (sorry!), keeping our online fundraising on track, ensuring our message reaches people on every platform, and yes, troubleshooting video takes from time to time. [_**If you already know you want to help us start December strong, here’s the link our team watches most closely:**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120760595/587279588/-431372808?amounts=5%2C10%2C15%2C25%2C50%2C100%2C250&amount=10&refcode=email_120625_boring&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83MjY1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZjY4OTFkOS1kZWQyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=l84J8aSgx7u77GFtR70PVuwMEsgy3XepEDS4iIulmIU=) | | [Donate Now →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120760596/587279589/1931420983?amounts=5%2C10%2C15%2C25%2C50%2C100%2C250&amount=10&refcode=email_120625_boring&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83MjY1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZjY4OTFkOS1kZWQyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=l84J8aSgx7u77GFtR70PVuwMEsgy3XepEDS4iIulmIU=)
---
_**Now I’ll explain why this email matters.**_
As someone who keeps an eye on our ActBlue numbers daily (and, fine, sometimes every minute), I can tell you our next FEC filing is coming up, and everything we raise online gets published for the whole world to review. Reporters, political operatives, outside groups, and especially the folks gearing up to run against John all study these filings to see if our grassroots team is as strong as it needs to be heading into 2026.
**Since John doesn’t take corporate PAC money, our report doesn’t hide who powers this campaign.** It’s you. It’s thousands of small donations. And it’s the level of enthusiasm people show during months exactly like this one.
That’s why December is so important. It sets the tone for the start of an election year, and it signals whether we have the resources to run a people-powered operation that can withstand the outside spending we know is coming.
[_**So if you’re able, even a small donation today makes a real impact on the report we’re preparing.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120760597/587279590/1931420983?amounts=5%2C10%2C15%2C25%2C50%2C100%2C250&amount=10&refcode=email_120625_boring&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83MjY1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZjY4OTFkOS1kZWQyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=l84J8aSgx7u77GFtR70PVuwMEsgy3XepEDS4iIulmIU=)

clean_body: but it’s actually pretty important for Colorado = Hi team, Jacob here. I’m the Digital Director for John Hickenlooper’s campaign. My job is a lot of what goes on behind the curtain: sending texts and emails like this one (sorry!), keeping our online fundraising on track, ensuring our message reaches people on every platform, and yes, troubleshooting video takes from time to time. = | | = Now I’ll explain why this email matters. As someone who keeps an eye on our ActBlue numbers daily (and, fine, sometimes every minute), I can tell you our next FEC filing is coming up, and everything we raise online gets published for the whole world to review. Reporters, political operatives, outside groups, and especially the folks gearing up to run against John all study these filings to see if our grassroots team is as strong as it needs to be heading into 2026. Since John doesn’t take corporate PAC money, our report doesn’t hide who powers this campaign. It’s you. It’s thousands of small donations. And it’s the level of enthusiasm people show during months exactly like this one. That’s why December is so important. It sets the tone for the start of an election year, and it signals whether we have the resources to run a people-powered operation that can withstand the outside spending we know is coming. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | = | = | | | = | = | | | = | = Thanks for sticking with us through the holidays and the “off-years.” It isn’t glamorous, but it’s the backbone of how we’ll keep Colorado’s Senate seat blue next year. subject: ultra rare combination Democrats desperately need
body:
| | | | | [](https://e96gjzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001n3IqJAnE6ioksGNHqjZVQ3LTPE12OJiC22Z-KcpLy-WIxlllHc4u1kSGPX2qf4UbQ-mylqVUgySbC3O46KC5V8f_AxgKjG115VCE-onzek8oiJrL8NUItHFhdu1G6Ti7_1xk3YYVW9a3kc6YxbNYf1PPwNyekcypoJj9q4rVmhyl7XfLzdfdUl_rKi6VebW4w6LVbiecEwj2C3pB7fXFqEGyXL-aRTVX&c=t8Nwm4Rlv4EAjf-rU2cZ38DuNRHAfjH_lfi3BOwGW-IiO6-gfsv62w==&ch=Q3M7p8Ual-jU2xLEONSBMFbm-K631v7MSzmqC7-e6gT4KaVUXikM5Q==)
---
|
Peter,
If 2025 has taught us anything, it's this: The Democrats are back.
Trump's approval is crashing. Even deep red districts are shifting blue by historic margins. The mandate is clear: voters want leaders who make life affordable, not chaotic.
Democrats have a rare opportunity right now: not just to take back the House from MAGA control, but to elect a new wave of Democratic leaders--a new generation ready to turn the page on the Trump era and begin to actually fix the issues facing our nation.
---
| | [](https://e96gjzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001n3IqJAnE6ioksGNHqjZVQ3LTPE12OJiC22Z-KcpLy-WIxlllHc4u1kSGPX2qf4UbQ-mylqVUgySbC3O46KC5V8f_AxgKjG115VCE-onzek8oiJrL8NUItHFhdu1G6Ti7_1xk3YYVW9a3kc6YxbNYf1PPwNyekcypoJj9q4rVmhyl7XfLzdfdUl_rKi6VebW4w6LVbiecEwgW3SDIkKpBLwUzsulCH8_p&c=t8Nwm4Rlv4EAjf-rU2cZ38DuNRHAfjH_lfi3BOwGW-IiO6-gfsv62w==&ch=Q3M7p8Ual-jU2xLEONSBMFbm-K631v7MSzmqC7-e6gT4KaVUXikM5Q==)
---
|
It starts in Illinois' 9th District with Bushra Amiwala.
Bushra is running to succeed Democrat Jan Schakowsky in one of the bluest seats in the country. But she isn't just looking to fill a seat; she's building a movement. At age 27, Bushra brings a rare combination of youth and experience. She was the first woman of her generation to be elected to public office and has served on the school board for one of Chicago's largest school districts for 7 years.
---
| [](https://e96gjzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001n3IqJAnE6ioksGNHqjZVQ3LTPE12OJiC22Z-KcpLy-WIxlllHc4u1kSGPX2qf4UbQ-mylqVUgySbC3O46KC5V8f_AxgKjG115VCE-onzek8oiJrL8NUItHFhdu1G6Ti7_1xk3YYVW9a3kc6YxbNYf1PPwNyekcypoJj9q4rVmhyl7XfLzdfdUl_rKi6VebW4w6LVbiecEwhKwRfPY9-SqR73byyHBoyj&c=t8Nwm4Rlv4EAjf-rU2cZ38DuNRHAfjH_lfi3BOwGW-IiO6-gfsv62w==&ch=Q3M7p8Ual-jU2xLEONSBMFbm-K631v7MSzmqC7-e6gT4KaVUXikM5Q==)
---
|
Bushra knows politics as usual is failing our neighbors, which is why she is fighting for universal health care, tuition-free college, debt forgiveness, and affordable housing.
But because she refuses corporate and special interest money, she has a target on her back.
Our campaign is facing down MAGA billionaires and AIPAC who have jumped into this race to try and prevent Bushra from winning. They are terrified of a young, progressive woman they can't buy.
While they have deep pockets, our campaign has something more powerful: the people. [Please, can we count on you to chip in $25, $10, or whatever is meaningful to you to help elect Bushra and help Democrats seize on this rare opportunity?](https://e96gjzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001n3IqJAnE6ioksGNHqjZVQ3LTPE12OJiC22Z-KcpLy-WIxlllHc4u1kSGPX2qf4Ubqc4m8xqGgPfnAsJNUT_rs_C3GPC68dgLUXWkNI4FNPXrKE4iagDM30qOBBP8OxiRxDkA6_h1YbdfYS5digC6cZL8_oxx6BFc0_1lBtW1-F7ftZg1gb3xuxKEqklOTlTBxxLxFo1yMaM9SK4uXMwkkSfmdQ8pN0f8J8o92aXnI30=&c=t8Nwm4Rlv4EAjf-rU2cZ38DuNRHAfjH_lfi3BOwGW-IiO6-gfsv62w==&ch=Q3M7p8Ual-jU2xLEONSBMFbm-K631v7MSzmqC7-e6gT4KaVUXikM5Q==)

The mandate is clear: voters want leaders who make life affordable, not chaotic. Democrats have a rare opportunity right now: not just to take back the House from MAGA control, but to elect a new wave of Democratic leaders--a new generation ready to turn the page on the Trump era and begin to actually fix the issues facing our nation. | | == | It starts in Illinois' 9th District with Bushra Amiwala. Bushra is running to succeed Democrat Jan Schakowsky in one of the bluest seats in the country. But she isn't just looking to fill a seat; she's building a movement. At age 27, Bushra brings a rare combination of youth and experience. She was the first woman of her generation to be elected to public office and has served on the school board for one of Chicago's largest school districts for 7 years. | == | Bushra knows politics as usual is failing our neighbors, which is why she is fighting for universal health care, tuition-free college, debt forgiveness, and affordable housing. But because she refuses corporate and special interest money, she has a target on her back. Our campaign is facing down MAGA billionaires and AIPAC who have jumped into this race to try and prevent Bushra from winning. They are terrified of a young, progressive woman they can't buy. | | | | | [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0011xARwgPyL96yXs0UbvIKUWEZsQVM1CrJR2zRWbGfH81Gn80XWCRPb8cu1vdeOBmvd8kmZii5z0GWvuZLiJB1mSOlIgYwR9Fe_SgpxGwIj3zjOVU5iuZZuZ2vsNowpu58AhX3P38H0g4vQwVRYj0iNliIWaGZ7nocP31FaeSJhAXGKGaoIKS5rk-ON4tgON_55uQ2ylfLyozABjbJwRpWco38MUjcf3gy&c=uXFGtlEth6IhFw5w5qmAS39IuVI93fWnZ0zCc-f7vFNvUNE3x9W7ow==&ch=dLYFcCGhkBP-ZrLHOvNwi3emEqzQMYTspYAan4UyKROOWCa03ZcbvQ==)

Peter,
We have long called for all of the Epstein Files to be released to the public. That should include any and all photo evidence, written communications, and especially the client list that Epstein and Ghislaine Maxwell kept to track what big name celebrities (and politicians) "partied" on the island.
Strangely, since Donald Trump begrudgingly signed the Epstein discharge petition last month, Lauren Boebert doesn't seem terribly interested in transparency, according to her own social media.
Boebert got oddly defensive after House Oversight Democrats released new photos showing private areas of Epstein's island mansion, melting down about the release and falsely claiming they were old photos (they aren't):

Lauren Boebert can't be trusted to do the right thing. She is a very sick person.
Carpetbagging Boebert has switched back to her default MAGA Mode to protect Trump--because without Trump's support, she stands NO chance against her hometown, Democratic opponent, Trisha Calvarese, who has consistently outraised Boebert each fundraising quarter.
A category 5 storm is brewing for Republicans like Lauren Boebert who keep following Trump off a cliff like loyal mindless lemmings.
Boebert only survived re-election by 546 votes. In 2024, her Democratic opponent Trisha Calvarese had the best performance a Democrat has ever had in this district, overperforming Kamala Harris by 6 pts while Boebert embarrassingly underperformed Trump in every single county.
[The only way we will ever get accountability and transparency over the Trump Administration is by defeating Lauren Boebert and flipping the House. If Boebert truly supported the Epstein Files release, why would she get so upset when new information is made public?
| | | | | [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001Bu7eag88rrWHNVOxATRmrSHLrUuEBLYjz6UcUvLdu1XbkFD8uhDAvKdEka947GUWljvYhrYNrYA3I3TtafgOU5eDlbpCeJCej5WBwdMYfQjKG9XExGJPz60jW2WZjOoLjhGpIAo8aynz_FzydUJUZDchxD3eJJCWqYvtFbne3-c9tzB0N2hcXlgoqX0aLliKGpsEsj768NN2ugWtT458kSjUsuxsPmpr&c=LqQMCj4TLkfFrnNZPN4JAvQWXhG7AregAxEmvcK2Dm-tk8_xnRVZ_w==&ch=aL5KQMDy10k1i2NkPObUfofMKII4qpX1yvCQ-WeybpgjDHnvFlZjKg==)
---
|

Peter,
We have long called for all of the Epstein Files to be released to the public. That should include any and all photo evidence, written communications, and especially the client list that Epstein and Ghislaine Maxwell kept to track what big name celebrities (and politicians) "partied" on the island.
Strangely, since Donald Trump begrudgingly signed the Epstein discharge petition last month, Lauren Boebert doesn't seem terribly interested in transparency, according to her own social media.
Boebert got oddly defensive after House Oversight Democrats released new photos showing private areas of Epstein's island mansion, melting down about the release and falsely claiming they were old photos (they aren't):

---
| [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001Bu7eag88rrWHNVOxATRmrSHLrUuEBLYjz6UcUvLdu1XbkFD8uhDAvKdEka947GUWljvYhrYNrYA3I3TtafgOU5eDlbpCeJCej5WBwdMYfQjKG9XExGJPz60jW2WZjOoLjhGpIAo8aynz_FzydUJUZDchxD3eJJCWqYvtFbne3-c9tzB0N2hcXlgoqX0aLliKGpsEsj768NM74S2sd9S-KDWw24xFYAXP&c=LqQMCj4TLkfFrnNZPN4JAvQWXhG7AregAxEmvcK2Dm-tk8_xnRVZ_w==&ch=aL5KQMDy10k1i2NkPObUfofMKII4qpX1yvCQ-WeybpgjDHnvFlZjKg==)
---

Lauren Boebert can't be trusted to do the right thing. She is a very sick person.
Carpetbagging Boebert has switched back to her default MAGA Mode to protect Trump--because without Trump's support, she stands NO chance against her hometown, Democratic opponent, Trisha Calvarese, who has consistently outraised Boebert each fundraising quarter.
A category 5 storm is brewing for Republicans like Lauren Boebert who keep following Trump off a cliff like loyal mindless lemmings.
Boebert only survived re-election by 546 votes. In 2024, her Democratic opponent Trisha Calvarese had the best performance a Democrat has ever had in this district, overperforming Kamala Harris by 6 pts while Boebert embarrassingly underperformed Trump in every single county.
[The only way we will ever get accountability and transparency over the Trump Administration is by defeating Lauren Boebert and flipping the House. Will you chip in a contribution to Trisha today to help her finally get rid of Lauren Boebert?](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001Bu7eag88rrWHNVOxATRmrSHLrUuEBLYjz6UcUvLdu1XbkFD8uhDAvKdEka947GUWljvYhrYNrYA3I3TtafgOU5eDlbpCeJCej5WBwdMYfQjKG9XExGJPz60jW2WZjOoLjhGpIAo8aynz_FzydUJUZDchxD3eJJCWqYvtFbne3-c9tzB0N2hcXlgoqX0aLliKGpsEsj768NM5xAP_n5CDHfSOnC93qHxZ&c=LqQMCj4TLkfFrnNZPN4JAvQWXhG7AregAxEmvcK2Dm-tk8_xnRVZ_w==&ch=aL5KQMDy10k1i2NkPObUfofMKII4qpX1yvCQ-WeybpgjDHnvFlZjKg==)

subject: The plot to rig the 2026 midterms

| Peter,
Soldiers are in our streets. ICE is kidnapping our neighbors from their homes. Grocery prices are skyrocketing, healthcare costs have spiraled out of control, and our economy sits at the brink of collapse.
The first year of Trump's presidency has been a disaster and Americans are pissed.
---
| [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001ibMy9zw9vqaqu6rs-HuJQDPhpzmiz2gyV4oYqJTVZ3gd4Qz2I05tx5SSrnRi5i5vCHztOKPqJr7YUPq_j_dIHJ7MxCf1fg-H8pkwHBnGlIDgK4DKDA4zzU5-YzXGuiIsueMf9bpvELjJcT_GkQT4r6hoyI2dbYBHfRETMQJKArrWdsurBUhXcAn9gra3Je3x3ZaQqgNfWMcztqDVfa-84B8E1hVA9JPZ&c=ZmcaycJJDZb1VDkaZ77ZIgX615mH2wAcN3gjQKJYz98gjRoY2LQZiA==&ch=Cn41SV0yCPR96v90wQagjKgi9wkZWGV92yeZuZu62qo-c6wrO4kXaQ==)
---
Meanwhile, Trump couldn't care less--he's throwing lavish Great Gatsby-themed parties for the elite at Mar-a-Lago, demolishing the East Wing to erect a ballroom in his name, and even remodeling the White House in gaudy, gold-plated decor.
Despite historic unpopularity and massive losses in elections across the country in November, Trump continues to practically mock Americans.
He sees the polls. He hears the backlash. But he isn't fazed. That's because Trump and MAGA are planning to cheat to keep their stranglehold on power, the will of the people be damned.
---
| [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001ibMy9zw9vqaqu6rs-HuJQDPhpzmiz2gyV4oYqJTVZ3gd4Qz2I05tx5SSrnRi5i5vCHztOKPqJr7YUPq_j_dIHJ7MxCf1fg-H8pkwHBnGlIDgK4DKDA4zzU5-YzXGuiIsueMf9bpvELjJcT_GkQT4r6hoyI2dbYBHfRETMQJKArrWdsurBUhXcAn9gra3Je3x3ZaQqgNfWMfwypjXqUMBfMpS6Cfwo7fF&c=ZmcaycJJDZb1VDkaZ77ZIgX615mH2wAcN3gjQKJYz98gjRoY2LQZiA==&ch=Cn41SV0yCPR96v90wQagjKgi9wkZWGV92yeZuZu62qo-c6wrO4kXaQ==)
---

Trump's plot to rig 2026 is already in motion. Gerrymandered maps have now passed in Texas, North Carolina, and Missouri to lock in GOP control, and a push for redistricting is currently underway in Indiana and Florida with more red states soon to follow.
Last month, California voters approved a ballot measure to fight fire with fire, countering the GOP's schemes. But that math alone isn't enough to overcome the red-state onslaught.
That is where Fair Fight Colorado comes in: we are launching the ballot measure that will give every Coloradan a chance to vote on a new Congressional map to level the playing field and protect our democracy.
We have the power to stop Trump's power grab, but we need to move fast.
Getting this on the ballot requires an astronomically expensive push for signatures and a statewide campaign to combat the lies MAGA is about to spew. They will throw everything they have at stopping us because they know this map is the firewall against their permanent majority.
[We need you in this fight with us: will you rush a contribution of $50, $25, or whatever you can spare to help us fight fire with fire?](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001ibMy9zw9vqaqu6rs-HuJQDPhpzmiz2gyV4oYqJTVZ3gd4Qz2I05tx5SSrnRi5i5vlKG3cUCUEKHXOQxyFCd3JNfEYxJNrOrawnIirIIsYpsUkquVOCKd-IuvmPeo8gW7Ecz-EkeesQVpkhI0-J5Cq3U-v0dq1ymx8YH3as0UEdsLlwU1x2mqC-X3jf94at8x535usx0FqVx177fDeK28xTxO0zUWkJaksL_U_-wSSuc=&c=ZmcaycJJDZb1VDkaZ77ZIgX615mH2wAcN3gjQKJYz98gjRoY2LQZiA==&ch=Cn41SV0yCPR96v90wQagjKgi9wkZWGV92yeZuZu62qo-c6wrO4kXaQ==)

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. To permanently honor the patriots who founded and carried the entire MAGA movement from Day One! The ones who never wavered, never surrendered, and refused to let them destroy our country twice.
I had my team pull the very best files, millions of patriot names, and I went through them myself.

clean_body: President Trump requests your presence in the 47th President Founders Club. | | | | EXCLUSIVE OFFER FROM THE DESK OF DONALD J. TRUMP Dear Patriot, President Trump requests your presence in the 47th President Founders Club. You’re not asleep! But yes, most people would dream of receiving an email like this one. I personally created the 47th President Founders Club for one reason only: To permanently honor the patriots who founded and carried the entire MAGA movement from Day One! The ones who never wavered, never surrendered, and refused to let them destroy our country twice. I had my team pull the very best files, millions of patriot names, and I went through them myself. Your name rose straight to the top. ** That’s why I’m personally inviting you to become a Charter Member of my 47th President Founders Club. Only 4,700 spots exist in the entire country. I’m holding one for you! ** Don’t miss your shot to stand with the patriots who made the greatest comeback in American history. Your name could be one of them. I am counting on you. Don’t let me down. Thank you and God bless you, President Trump | | | | | | | | | | | | Contributions to Trump National Committee JFC Inc. are not deductible for federal income tax purposes. name: Justice Democrats
subject: She’s been in Congress for Melat’s entire life
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Justice Democrats
body:
If you want to serve in Congress, you have to actually make things better.
#
Peter,
Justice Democrats just endorsed Melat Kiros in CO-01 because we need leaders who aren't afraid to challenge entrenched incumbents backed by corporate Super PACs — especially those who do it while calling themselves “progressive.”
Let us tell you why this race for the future of Denver matters and why Melat’s opponent has to go. **[But first, if you’re ready to take out a Big Pharma darling, split a donation between Melat’s campaign and Justice Democrats right now>>](https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251206-mk.fFSXRp&refcode=em20251206-37257&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=37257%2E1203867%2EpNk1GD)**
Melat is running against Diana DeGette, _who has served in Congress for the entirety of Melat’s life._ She calls herself progressive, but is bankrolled by corporate PACs. She wants her voters to think she’ll support Medicare for All, but is powered by Big Pharma.
After decades in office, rents keep rising. Medical bills are getting more expensive. And child care now costs more than some people’s paychecks. DeGette doesn’t think she has to campaign for her seat, so we are bringing a fight to her.
Melat is completely rejecting corporate PAC money because she believes leadership means being accountable to people, not lobbyists. Instead of doing what massive corporations want, she’ll support legislation that helps every Denver family: **Medicare for All, Housing First, and Universal Childcare.[Split $5 now if you’re ready to help us send Melat to Congress.](https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251206-mk.fFSXRp&refcode=em20251206-37257&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=3&akid=37257%2E1203867%2EpNk1GD)**
If we’re going to win these policies, then we need leaders who don’t just sign on to bills and expect a gold star while taking corporate PAC checks under the table. We need leaders whose entire job is to fight for bold solutions to our most urgent crises. But the lobbies that Melat’s opponent works for won’t go down without a fight. We need to make sure Melat has the grassroots resources she needs to run an effective campaign and win.
**[Peter, if you’re with us, can you split a donation of $5 between Melat’s campaign and Justice Democrats? This race is a real opportunity for us to unseat the type of corporate Democrat that is stopping real progress for working-class Americans.](https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251206-mk.fFSXRp&refcode=em20251206-37257&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=4&akid=37257%2E1203867%2EpNk1GD)**
**[Contribute $5](https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251206-mk.fFSXRp&refcode=em20251206-37257&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=23&akid=37257%2E1203867%2EpNk1GD)**
In solidarity,
Justice Democrats

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20251206-37257-footer&t=24&refcode2=37257_1203867_pNk1GD&akid=37257%2E1203867%2EpNk1GD)**

As we head into the holidays, most organizations are winding down. But here at EMILYs List? We're gearing UP — because the work we do right now determines how fast we can hit the ground running in 2026.

And there's no time to waste.

[**Before we dive into everything we've got planned for December, donate today to fuel the fight:**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120761527/587282867/-1403821653?refcode=WUEFEL12_20251206_DecToplines_ALL_v1s1&sourceid=1057411&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1OTQ2OGE4Ny1lNmQyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qkdBveU6-Q7eEcsQgbFVdFQQkfCyFz0Ewok5EJkg91o=) | [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120761528/587282870/-1403821653?refcode=WUEFEL12_20251206_DecToplines_ALL_v1s1&sourceid=1057411&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1OTQ2OGE4Ny1lNmQyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qkdBveU6-Q7eEcsQgbFVdFQQkfCyFz0Ewok5EJkg91o=)

Here's where we're headed:
**🏃♀️ Runoff season is here: **The runoff election for Houston's U.S. House seat is on January 31, and we're ready to power **Amanda Edwards (TX-18)** over the finish line. Miami's mayoral runoff is on December 9, and early voting for **Eileen Higgins** is already underway. These races don't take holiday breaks — so neither do we.

**🔵 Our 2026 blueprint is expanding: **We're rolling out updated plans and scaling up our boots-on-the-ground operation earlier than ever. Plus, we're endorsing powerhouse women across the country who are ready to defend our freedoms and flip critical seats.

**🪪 Membership for 2026 is coming: **Keep an eye on your inbox. You'll soon be able to vote on new membership card designs, weigh in on our 2026 priorities, and join the team that's building our biggest election cycle yet.
This is how we win tough runoff races now and set the stage for taking back power in 2026. But the plan only works if we have the resources to keep moving at full speed, even during the holidays. That's how we've built the strongest bench of Democratic pro-choice women in history.

clean_body: This is how we win tough runoff races now and set the stage for taking back power in 2026. | = Hi Peter, As we head into the holidays, most organizations are winding down. But here at EMILYs List? We're gearing UP — because the work we do right now determines how fast we can hit the ground running in 2026. And there's no time to waste. = | = Here's where we're headed: 🏃♀️ Runoff season is here: The runoff election for Houston's U.S. House seat is on January 31, and we're ready to power Amanda Edwards (TX-18) over the finish line. Miami's mayoral runoff is on December 9, and early voting for Eileen Higgins is already underway. These races don't take holiday breaks — so neither do we. 🔵 Our 2026 blueprint is expanding: We're rolling out updated plans and scaling up our boots-on-the-ground operation earlier than ever. Plus, we're endorsing powerhouse women across the country who are ready to defend our freedoms and flip critical seats. 🪪 Membership for 2026 is coming: Keep an eye on your inbox. You'll soon be able to vote on new membership card designs, weigh in on our 2026 priorities, and join the team that's building our biggest election cycle yet. This is how we win tough runoff races now and set the stage for taking back power in 2026. But the plan only works if we have the resources to keep moving at full speed, even during the holidays. That's how we've built the strongest bench of Democratic pro-choice women in history. **=** If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue, your donation will go through immediately: = | = ---|--- = | = = | = – the EMILYs List Campaigns team = | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- Thank you for being a supporter of EMILYs List. This email was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com. Inbox overcrowded? Sign up to get less email . We'd hate to see you go, but if you want to stop receiving all email, unsubscribe . Have a question? Feel free to drop us a line at mailto:information@emilyslist.org or simply reply to this email. Contributions or gifts to EMILYs List or endorsed candidates are not tax deductible. EMILYs List P.O. Box 96612 Washington, DC 20077 United States | ---|---

name: Pass the No Kings Act (VPP)
subject: Are Democrats giving up?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
It’s not too late to turn this around:
| Friend, our momentum to pass the No Kings Act has slowed down since the protests on October 18th... [](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/98138?t=6&akid=22139%2E878179%2EXhS3wU) _So here are your options:_

**OPTION 1:** GIVE UP on holding Trump accountable and let him anoint himself an untouchable king above the law. No action is required to select this option.

**OPTION 2:** Get back in the fight to SAVE our democracy, REVERSE the Supreme Court’s disastrous immunity ruling, and stop Trump before it’s too late!

[**To select Option 2, click here >>**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/98138?t=7&akid=22139%2E878179%2EXhS3wU)

The choice is yours, friend. We can limit our collective power to a single day and a single protest, _or we can keep fighting with everything we’ve got._

[**If you’re with us, join 500 Democrats in rushing $7 by 11:59pm to help PASS the No Kings Act and say NO to King Trump! >>**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/98138?t=8&akid=22139%2E878179%2EXhS3wU) If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | [CONTRIBUTE $7 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/98139?t=9&akid=22139%2E878179%2EXhS3wU)
---
[CONTRIBUTE $25 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/98140?t=10&akid=22139%2E878179%2EXhS3wU)
[CONTRIBUTE $50 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/98141?t=11&akid=22139%2E878179%2EXhS3wU)
[CONTRIBUTE $100 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/98142?t=12&akid=22139%2E878179%2EXhS3wU)
[Or a custom donation »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/98143?t=13&akid=22139%2E878179%2EXhS3wU)
Let’s do this,

The Voter Protection Project
| | Paid for by VPP
(www.protectvoting.org)
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=22139%2E878179%2EXhS3wU). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=22139%2E878179%2EXhS3wU).**

[Click here](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=22139%2E878179%2EXhS3wU) to request fewer emails. Click here to [unsubscribe](https://act.protectvoting.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=14&akid=22139%2E878179%2EXhS3wU). | | [Please: Pitch In Once A Month To Protect Voting Rights »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/123?t=4&akid=22139%2E878179%2EXhS3wU)
---
[Donate Just One Time »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/124?t=5&akid=22139%2E878179%2EXhS3wU)

clean_body: Confirm that you’re by our side >> | Republican State Leadership Allies List Name: Fellow American Email: Response Deadline: MIDNIGHT Fellow American, we noticed you've been quiet lately, and we're getting concerned. The Radical Left is doing everything in their power to dismantle the America First agenda, and we need your help to defend it. Their out-of-touch policies are a direct attack on our American values, and it's crucial we stand strong against their attempts to undermine our nation. It is more important now more than ever to know who has our back in this fight. Knowing that patriots such as yourself are with us has been CRUCIAL as we work to stop the Radical Left at all levels of government, and we can't afford to lose your support now. * By providing your mobile phone number, you are consenting to receive calls and texts and P2P messages, including autodialed and automated calls and texts, to that number from RSLC and its affiliated PACs. Message frequency may vary. Msg&Data Rates May Apply. Reply STOP to cancel. Reply HELP for help. Contributions or gifts to the Republican State Leadership Committee (RSLC) and its affiliated PACs are not tax deductible. Contributions may be used for activities of the RSLC and its affiliated PACs at the RSLC's sole discretion. The RSLC does not accept earmarked contributions. Contributions from foreign nationals are not solicited or permitted. | | | Paid for by Republican State Leadership Committee 1201 F Street, NW, Suite 675 Washington, DC 20004 | To ensure that you don't miss out on important messages, please add "info@email.gop-govs.com" to your address book. Not Sure How? Follow the link below to your email provider to get step-by-step instructions on how to add us to your address book. , , , ,

subject: What I’d tell you over coffee
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY VIVEK RAMASWAMY FOR OHIO
body:
I will never take your sacrifice for granted.
[](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=1d39678bbb5449e7ab3572516c022e83&_e=h3skiVzTIy4T8pbuinW0b1L_2pdpmstvNTxlAk5bCR5EefeKYxFv9y3IEN_zrFTy-ROwPHyFpYps8x_RPZDxvViZ0Ke_v1p91dZGqKVSO_gNwS82BlncAw0CpZSTJXeP-3t2iMURMADV-mjO1mq2k9ggDte8rK1SNqYgR0OYmhgbaPaAbx5DvF8vJ_KnBnJ_X15s-HAEDxT8NPk2Nwa3AfhNzZ1QtTD54CNj2tdhw2IXgF8SvQuozzG94S8tI0tqdYO2-lt3TLaqoBJQOxbvX0GYad6-2Q-f0bHBf3WQywAt51x5PhzaB0aREeFjgpqxG7RnZyAFOvClemk85VozL68Z5OCREhW_V059MINKoj9vohosXOjXYeumjQ8WRlyhOMSPJdduApiTr_R-0T2atlGG_LBNVHRLeLqtEbRKzNMd8Zerjm5D8xhWh4wZX-aDeEBGU3A2xaOQNFoeumYoLZyGotK_mrHdhGOthHeXM6I2ieJW3Ue63pQiIHrUIJj7D_wclGgz_w01n_UsPiMic_eqMrEoGOQyjMjL2czeWANOBvVSSPCHyDAajCfwQvG1TDb9igcMxHjvTMudAXq1xZUP4vnoiW4Uy2eMsh8p4OuVzdrH_7Rt9m49ueH6V-DOEJImO8LyoaLs-iXD68bO632CsJYSc6FsWwGNELk26A4zz6AARwBIrszhZHb1KWYp96JdjcCVODbjM0RWIqGsEN03BmIfTwEUROEVJEiAzffi6V4hdUh1Ql6lzHF9fggUlTOTjNhC40IIStAaJpORvbpk7US9B-n349VH5UemzUfoMbdqlYxPayZWcdUbxzRnpOi5Ap6EozCmBnXrpJwTt8Y0XhvKmHwILuxkz0E4Ply6Hlf0AB2_A0osScHi-hEi6EtsroPbnYWH_oucWVMUELoPIkSlI_xYR8RU8aAwiJY%3D)Politics today is full of noise - phony deadlines, scare tactics, and cheap gimmicks.
But I want to cut through the noise for a moment.
[I’d like to tell you a quick story.](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=1d39678bbb5449e7ab3572516c022e83&_e=h3skiVzTIy4T8pbuinW0b1L_2pdpmstvNTxlAk5bCR5EefeKYxFv9y3IEN_zrFTy-ROwPHyFpYps8x_RPZDxvViZ0Ke_v1p91dZGqKVSO_gNwS82BlncAw0CpZSTJXeP-3t2iMURMADV-mjO1mq2k9ggDte8rK1SNqYgR0OYmhgbaPaAbx5DvF8vJ_KnBnJ_X15s-HAEDxT8NPk2Nwa3AfhNzZ1QtTD54CNj2tdhw2IXgF8SvQuozzG94S8tI0tqdYO2-lt3TLaqoBJQOxbvX0GYad6-2Q-f0bHBf3WQywAt51x5PhzaB0aREeFjgpqxG7RnZyAFOvClemk85VozL68Z5OCREhW_V059MINKoj8ESDIeccd133EFEDuSoXxO9w-ndyZ8CGGIS9uGrtTkXVek66tWgUwmnWYakul-Sdhb1bpW0ASyoxSDpWV27AKAB47Xi_rmDk_LbhB5xH6BKRLBOwKebkcYB0VxB7RL_97PYUE2tcJkws-kpB9L-cbNN_L_kmz4PYWr6PIrpB9tckETznBGcvY4Pvk0gBCxQMZ5tPDTkzAnmizn0mvj8ZOcjKR6A2WHJGrzawoqmU43cNrRuttDi5nqxx27ubE7gtpzfgcdJ9rF4nAFRxS4PZQDpXdcgz7vwlHaHTbjfuuTGDZAEuh2DQlfKqMIAbuveyL8jPd9dDJRCFsfa8LELn5NsK1IYdOWeektk3BjPewTC0ML1LUwWkJpxwr_waTQFJjB5lju045-bKjMOIDUxyOrQifhHObW_JUbynjT88SeWPqNXUMYJMo6Nognkc9KxT0sGIUw2i-K4_klCJEtvXpzqE6-2nQdE3nvW1YZodpyNdlkvVPMDpSGTt7m3U0P546tdqxhTyPTWDGRNUbEpHceVvYAnOGM5buz0vGl-TUfDKcVtnMe7KccINOYYYmCrig%3D)
I grew up right here in Ohio. My parents worked hard to give me a chance at the American Dream, and **I know what sacrifice looks like**. Every opportunity I've had was built on the back of that sacrifice.[](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=1d39678bbb5449e7ab3572516c022e83&_e=h3skiVzTIy4T8pbuinW0b1L_2pdpmstvNTxlAk5bCR5EefeKYxFv9y3IEN_zrFTy-ROwPHyFpYps8x_RPZDxvViZ0Ke_v1p91dZGqKVSO_gNwS82BlncAw0CpZSTJXeP-3t2iMURMADV-mjO1mq2k9ggDte8rK1SNqYgR0OYmhgbaPaAbx5DvF8vJ_KnBnJ_X15s-HAEDxT8NPk2Nwa3AfhNzZ1QtTD54CNj2tdhw2IXgF8SvQuozzG94S8tI0tqdYO2-lt3TLaqoBJQOxbvX0GYad6-2Q-f0bHBf3WQywAt51x5PhzaB0aREeFjgpqxG7RnZyAFOvClemk85VozL68Z5OCREhW_V059MINKoj-lbAxecvRE5AWUG3Aod2jXNcKnRr8iWi-Y39fFdTQH4_NkwAx4vUbPQJDSzty3piXZKnSMFBwLuun8EiytRW787f022RjzAhRgH1WtFcywiKnJnUiUa_ZH51Br9b2W8S1xXQDHJYNJnoV8MHfFu-_KRE69jMMt0vNmRD8QY7Yx7jUghrZNjn4A9MDipiN1BT7c6gIXFM0QkVRJeYS8glov2B6-zQEQcKu-dlcHRc_b_x1Hq5AjrqAiJoVimA-wR50WtuN9YS_PLV0tWP5VZT71LvTSscVACN_JYNqLrujQXhr7HyxVtlVW1KJugDkY51OosrKvfoAzV64TxlgeGM82t40OG373OQ_wehUTAjHLh2t2CcVSHIXqSYVWw92y6up7Y7yh_VnRF6EtyiMAcfRr5r5B03trKc5Imftoy6VzZONBA-eR0s7GUXdLXwneVr2Z2_GnFClcHc0WNSqUKN86Rb59KGzDGgstRRUvq0Xk_beW7sfIXHS5ryOlAUxPZryPm4nSKKlcS70PLZPMrCxLhb9wo4H8w8EDXfh1gQdnomChWZhPsBtcZFTpCkpO2-c%3D)That's why I never forget what it means when someone like you chips in a few dollars. It isn't "just money." **
**It 's trust. It's belief. It's a vote of confidence in the movement we're building in Ohio and beyond. **
**So whether you 've already donated or you're considering it today, let me say this clearly: **
**[THANK YOU, Fellow American!](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=1d39678bbb5449e7ab3572516c022e83&_e=h3skiVzTIy4T8pbuinW0b1L_2pdpmstvNTxlAk5bCR5EefeKYxFv9y3IEN_zrFTy-ROwPHyFpYps8x_RPZDxvViZ0Ke_v1p91dZGqKVSO_gNwS82BlncAw0CpZSTJXeP-3t2iMURMADV-mjO1mq2k9ggDte8rK1SNqYgR0OYmhgbaPaAbx5DvF8vJ_KnBnJ_X15s-HAEDxT8NPk2Nwa3AfhNzZ1QtTD54CNj2tdhw2IXgF8SvQuozzG94S8tI0tqdYO2-lt3TLaqoBJQOxbvX0GYad6-2Q-f0bHBf3WQywAt51x5PhzaB0aREeFjgpqxG7RnZyAFOvClemk85VozL68Z5OCREhW_V059MINKoj_xZj8HjSNNM4I326Zl4OOvv0OtvnFrtownmM3dp6OqzvtqpOb8SDK1lLLPYnLJVLY1TAJwXLbNCjblc3U3dF6GrLjas6HApchKMp0Mc22zrToNxE26yZrIKVMtCQLzreofXQ056NUJGOybT-bd5F8xH7F41UAc97MkyUvSlJL9Zp6ORAOsUHf6ie1HjIThf1cH2ZWuuUWtlPKI2qel43VjR_TL83SVx0QHtelre81QdA40IK3PXihPVHBCntO3mAY4-D4vGkyGYSwoDCf_Mctw2HRWXNuu7zXgbSqatXqHO5EkYu9U5loLiG0Rchfl_yty6_E_onkDLXz0OYZwWRaLI5oN9yhjghr5z7ZrHI-PyqA6A0zoiZOLcCpEaWN5uVGrU4st2PeJ_NvOQ4QqpzyyRqKHNivrPWJRgIYNSGL9FsmaaR-ethB2rqNxai6UoqCA7dHZemKPHqhKTcwWn0HFXe27b9EODDXpZXZFmyblmh68Pv6Sxz4z7MrYi1J2cj5bxALc_E-ung_oUmlx2PSQvZwEGaWmrUAoWLy_9GJU8rZAAd7kTtapqs8IESzSWsM%3D)**
**We 're in an extremely tough fight. And this isn't just any race - it's shaping up to be one of the most important in the country.**
**I threw my hat in the ring now because we have a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to save our country. I mean that. We were[ ONE ELECTION AWAY](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=1d39678bbb5449e7ab3572516c022e83&_e=h3skiVzTIy4T8pbuinW0b1L_2pdpmstvNTxlAk5bCR5EefeKYxFv9y3IEN_zrFTy-ROwPHyFpYps8x_RPZDxvViZ0Ke_v1p91dZGqKVSO_gNwS82BlncAw0CpZSTJXeP-3t2iMURMADV-mjO1mq2k9ggDte8rK1SNqYgR0OYmhgbaPaAbx5DvF8vJ_KnBnJ_X15s-HAEDxT8NPk2Nwa3AfhNzZ1QtTD54CNj2tdhw2IXgF8SvQuozzG94S8tI0tqdYO2-lt3TLaqoBJQOxbvX0GYad6-2Q-f0bHBf3WQywAt51x5PhzaB0aREeFjgpqxG7RnZyAFOvClemk85VozL68Z5OCREhW_V059MINKoj8x5v2Y6FYpqda1rANI22dC2Kjd3jT8YFFsmhntsdnrBm8EdONoD_CFI3usGvdk0V-BJ-j7YoDa74Uf0BbRBNEh3WBKLPEOmONu3NLgfQsIKJL4ZH-XHbpqjUiXNbY3QEmsklQPB5V7N_Nag42wyWI2tHWRrGK6R6yIp2pO2OOZXIy0V30nXdLz766_rGM7lEvE7pKgDlwMlabj_eOPyhTVGqjg6PYS6f6IaWODMNBMfwZryLE8apVipNR8w0fEOrsN0tMFm1CY7dvdAOKYTyAbqhlaoHUUkl14wyzb692DeHL4Hdw-0M78AgRieerpP0IGWAgZwW7ooMdvW-pSfDiEpB9HKJB9wrBJ7KlzcyIFsjBpAw-K0E3tVjLbS3M1DrdadDXss-lSyWuycLd35WNxN05c2GK-TXTGHH6jbJB2Oni49ABUhZ8GlyY61OUQ8zk8yHFRNQymb6QEssALsSd_OkhB6QuXdhcGU3d6njGQ8wGCYIZoOnIJO3wAXWOABqu0qWsRyb-Mw-4B063D4lUhBV3TyWERrAp1jJrBmk3x9ySGiixzOU-juq-AmiFqpx4%3D) from a country you and I wouldn't recognize anymore.**
**President Trump saved us - with the help of folks like you - from that dire outcome. With that said, he needs loyal partners at the state level to help him succeed. **

clean_body: I will never take your sacrifice for granted. today is full of noise - phony deadlines, scare tactics, and cheap gimmicks. But I want to cut through the noise for a moment. I grew up right here in Ohio. My parents worked hard to give me a chance at the American Dream, and I know what sacrifice looks like. Every opportunity I've had was built on the back of that sacrifice. why I never forget what it means when someone like you chips in a few dollars. It isn't "just money." It 's trust. It's belief. It's a vote of confidence in the movement we're building in Ohio and beyond. So whether you 've already donated or you're considering it today, let me say this clearly: We 're in an extremely tough fight. And this isn't just any race - it's shaping up to be one of the most important in the country. I threw my hat in the ring now because we have a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to save our country. I mean that. We were from a country you and I wouldn't recognize anymore. President Trump saved us - with the help of folks like you - from that dire outcome. With that said, he needs loyal partners at the state level to help him succeed. That 's why this race means so much. So if you 've given before (even just once, even just a buck) know this: you've already made a difference, and I will never take your sacrifice for granted. And if you haven 't given yet - - I'd be honored to have you on my team. Every single gift, no matter the size, fuels this must-win fight. grateful,Vivek Ramaswamy the election were held today, we 'd come up just short.We can't afford to lose this ground -- not now, not after everything we've built.Your support right now makes the difference between a comeback and a collapse.**| | | | PAID FOR BY VIVEK RAMASWAMY FOR OHIO 350 E 1st Ave Ste 100 Columbus, OH 43201

body:
Throwing workers under the bus? Not on my watch.

[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120761869/587283929/1239926708?refcode=em_WEI_FR_Misclassification_120625&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzNDBjNjE3MC1lY2QyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=G8HX9578QhN9mLuDSpLz23OvMQQN_xOEqQG01w_jSxI=&emci=ce4d3cda-2dd2-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=340c6170-ecd2-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=51844520) Friend, it’s Phil. We had a win for workers recently. I wanted to tell you about it because it shows what this campaign is all about: _listening, fighting, and delivering for every Coloradan._ **[If you feel ready right now to support our campaign for a more affordable and fair Colorado, I’d be grateful if you’d back us by chipping in $10 to my campaign today >>>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120761870/587283931/-198793374?amount=10&refcode=em_WEI_FR_Misclassification_120625&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzNDBjNjE3MC1lY2QyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=G8HX9578QhN9mLuDSpLz23OvMQQN_xOEqQG01w_jSxI=)** | | [CHIP IN $10](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120761871/587283932/-198793374?amount=10&refcode=em_WEI_FR_Misclassification_120625&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzNDBjNjE3MC1lY2QyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=G8HX9578QhN9mLuDSpLz23OvMQQN_xOEqQG01w_jSxI=)
---
**➡️ Here’s what happened:** A staffing agency in Colorado was misclassifying employees as independent contractors. That means their workers — and there were over 69,000 of them in the Denver area alone — were illegally deprived of their rights and legal protections. In this case, one of the worst problems was the agency skipping out on paying required unemployment insurance premiums, one of our most critical safety nets for when people are out of work.
It’s hard enough to lose your job. If companies don’t pay into unemployment insurance like they’re supposed to, it slashes holes in the safety net that helps keep families afloat while job hunting. **And for Coloradans struggling to get by, that just makes things even harder to deal with.**
This wasn’t right. So when we found out about it, we sprang into action.
➡️ **Here’s how we remedied it:** We took the staffing agency to court under 2022’s Colorado False Claims Act. **_And we held it accountable, forcing it to change its ways, treat workers properly, and pay hundreds of thousands of dollars in settlement funds._**
**Look, as your governor, you can always count on me to stand up for you if anyone tries to mistreat you on the job.** When I’m governor, we’ll support workers, bolster our economy, invest in our workforce, and address the affordability crisis. That means enforcing laws that ensure companies play by the rules, just like workers have to do.
**[A strong economy depends on workers being treated fairly, friend. If irresponsible companies get away with breaking the rules, we are putting responsible companies at an unfair disadvantage._I won’t let that happen._ If that’s a vision you can get behind, will you chip in $5, $25, or whatever works for you?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120761872/587283934/-198793374?amount=5&refcode=em_WEI_FR_Misclassification_120625&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzNDBjNjE3MC1lY2QyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=G8HX9578QhN9mLuDSpLz23OvMQQN_xOEqQG01w_jSxI=)**

In this case, one of the worst problems was the agency skipping out on paying required unemployment insurance premiums, one of our most critical safety nets for when people are out of work. It’s hard enough to lose your job. If companies don’t pay into unemployment insurance like they’re supposed to, it slashes holes in the safety net that helps keep families afloat while job hunting. And for Coloradans struggling to get by, that just makes things even harder to deal with. This wasn’t right. So when we found out about it, we sprang into action. ➡️ Here’s how we remedied it: We took the staffing agency to court under 2022’s Colorado False Claims Act. And we held it accountable, forcing it to change its ways, treat workers properly, and pay hundreds of thousands of dollars in settlement funds. Look, as your governor, you can always count on me to stand up for you if anyone tries to mistreat you on the job. When I’m governor, we’ll support workers, bolster our economy, invest in our workforce, and address the affordability crisis. That means enforcing laws that ensure companies play by the rules, just like workers have to do. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | = | = | | | = | = | | | = | = With gratitude, Phil | Make sure this email goes to your inbox. Add teamphil@philforcolorado.com to your address book. This campaign is powered by grassroots donations. Email is a key way for us to stay in touch and make sure you get the latest updates from our campaign. But if you would like to . | = | Paid for by Phil Weiser for Colorado. Anna Noschese, Registered Agent. Phil Weiser for Colorado PO Box 13644 Denver, CO 80201 United States

---

On a crystal blue sea, multiple explosive assaults from the sky have blown small boats to pieces, almost always ending the lives of those on board.
Only twice, to our knowledge, have survivors been identified as the smoke cleared. On September 2, 2025, two survivors were identified clinging to the wreckage of the boat when another strike was ordered that summarily ended the lives of those survivors.
And on the 17th of October, around 1430, or 2:30 PM, the smoke cleared from a strike and two survivors were identified. This time, those individuals were retrieved from the wreckage and brought on board the USS Iwo Jima.
What transpired after those individuals were brought on board proves that we are in an entirely new paradigm within the US military, one in which video and photographic material is deleted, US military servicemembers are asked to defy standing protocol and code, and human beings disappear from record as they are summarily and without trial returned to what is purported to be their native countries.
* * *
The hotly debated U.S. attacks on alleged narcotics trafficking boats have proven to be a series of grave missteps of this administration that have dominated news cycles for weeks. With breaking news of every additional strike, public opinion gets louder and demands for accountability are breaking through to Congress.
This week, a closed-door meeting of the Armed Services Committee reviewed even more evidence related to this "mission", some of which goes beyond the explosive review of video footage of one of those strikes.
'Extra-judicial' seems an appropriate summation, and countless experts have weighed in to clarify the finer points of military and international law, murky as it may sometimes seem.
Nick Paro, a former US Army Sergeant trained as a Human Intelligence Collector with advanced training in Interrogation & Analysis Techniques, compiled a thorough outline of the military and international law that governs the treatments of combatants of all classifications in a piece entitled "Wounded and Unknown: Treatment and Release Procedures for "Unprivileged Belligerents".
Nick Paro explains, "The DoD Manual treats Unprivileged Belligerents [UBs] as liable to capture and detention, but insists they receive humane treatment. Notably, when there is doubt about status at capture, the Manual requires granting POW protections until a _competent tribunal_ decides otherwise (emphasis his). Operationally, this boils down to the core components of: units _must_ document captures, units _must_ provide medical care, and units _must_ initiate status‐determination procedures while safeguarding force protection. Practical Implication:**** These operational steps provide an easily understood and repeatable process for properly handling in-processing, medical care, and detainment procedures of any captured person."1
The precise choice to designate these individuals as "Unprivileged Belligerents" is no mistake.
UBs have provided a grey loophole in established international law that enables the US to deny the full protections provided in the Geneva Convention for Prisoners of War _or_ Civilians.
Essentially, the US is attesting that every one of the victims of these strikes was, and is, to be considered an Unprivileged Belligerent. Retired Brigadier General of the Canadian Army Kenneth Watkin is a foremost expert on Combatants in International Conflicts, particularly the classification of Unprivileged Belligerents.
In 2003, for the International Humanitarian Law Research Initiative at Harvard University, Brig. General Watkin explains: "Exclusion of a group from combatant status is perhaps most easily applied in respect of terrorist organizations that by definition do not respect the fundamental distinction between combatants and civilians in their actions and sometimes overtly reject any requirement to do so."
He clarifies: "A decision to exclude a group from combatant and therefore POW status should not be based on an assessment of the "justness" of that group's cause. The application of "just war" thinking to combatant status is fraught with difficulty....It is a fundamental principle of international humanitarian law that the treatment of captured personnel is based on their meeting appropriate objective criteria and not the "justness" of the cause they serve."
Drawing to the point, Watkin concludes, "It has been noted that the decision to exclude a group from attaining combatant status should not be taken lightly. Such a determination may undermine the inherent incentive for the denied group to comply with international humanitarian law and might be perceived to be inconsistent with the humanitarian purpose of international humanitarian law."2
#### The importance of the designation "Unprivileged Belligerence"
On October 17, 2025, the two individuals who survived a boat strike were brought on board the USS Iwo Jima and began to be processed in accordance with standing military and international humanitarian law, including the capture of photo and video documentation.
According to a confidential document shared with us by an anonymous source, there was an immediate disagreement to even begin the photo and video documentation of the detainee processing. Shipboard personnel insisted on documenting, referring to standing policy on detainee onboarding procedure. The Marines who brought the detainees on board pushed back, citing the word of the Commodore that video and photography were prohibited.
The Public Affairs Officer prevailed and documentation ensued, both photo and video.
But less than two hours later, the order came down for that footage to be deleted. All of it.
Our anonymous source states that after much deliberation and conferring with JAG, the order was confirmed: delete all video and photographic documentation of the detainees. The shipboard personnel recorded their objections via a written statement after following through with the order.
In summation: two detainees were brought on board a US Navy vessel, one in apparent medical distress having survived an explosive boat strike. Shipboard personnel followed standing protocol and code to capture video and photo documentation of the onboarding of these individuals, and then were given the dubious order to delete that footage.
The reason given was this: those individuals had been classified as "Unprivileged Belligerents" and therefore deemed not worthy of the basic protections of photo and video footage of their treatment while in the care of the United States Navy and Marine Corps.
Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth has claimed that the act of driving and manning a boat with drugs navigating anywhere remotely in the direction of north, towards the United States, is an act of hostility at such a level it demands "ultimate lethality", making the individuals on those boats "combatants" rather than civilians. Although he has produced no evidence to Congress to justify these actions or the classification of these individuals, much less requesting and gaining approval for acts of hostility upon those individuals, he has then proceeded with lethal strikes on civilians in open international waters. If the evidence were as overwhelming as DoD claims, following established legislative, legal, and military protocol would not present a problem and would have been followed by SECDEF. His refusal to follow established protocols, code, and international law, as well as essentially redefining what constitutes "hostile action", places our entire military in an extraordinarily compromised position.
The Utrecht Law Review states: " If an individual (or category of individuals) qualifies as a prisoner of war, he, she is (or they are) by implication presumed to possess combatant status. In the event the qualifications for prisoner of war status are not met, the automatic presumption will be that the individual concerned is a civilian. ...However, if a civilian or other non-combatant engages in belligerent acts or participates directly in hostilities, he or she commits an offence and by implication becomes what is often referred to in the literature as an unlawful combatant. Should any doubt arise concerning the status of an individual and the treatment he or she is entitled to, the relevant provisions of the international humanitarian law instruments relating to prisoner of war status and treatment make it unequivocally clear that the individual(s) concerned is (are) entitled to the protections afforded by those instruments until such time as the status thereof has been determined by a competent tribunal."3
No evidence currently exists of any such competent tribunal being conducted in the frenzy of activity between the time the survivors were identified and pulled from the wreckage of the boat to their being onboarded the USS Iwo Jima. In fact, Marines and Sailors could not even agree on whether or not to engage in the standard protocol for onboarding detainees of obtaining video footage of said activity.
Brig. Gen. Watkin writes in 2003, "In respect of "unprivileged belligerents": ... it is possible to envisage a day when the law will be so re-tailored as to place all belligerents, however garbed, in a protected status."
In 2025, it appears that the United States Secretary of Defense is determined, as with many other projects he's undertaken, to regress our military to a barbaric status, where we shoot first and ask questions later, don't bother to provide evidence or due process to those we deem to be hostile combatants, and engage in lethal warfare against said individuals without offering them even the most basic standard humanitarian protections of the Geneva Convention.
The American public deserves and demands transparency when our flag flies on vessels launching missiles onto civilians at sea.
Video and photographic evidence would go a great distance to alleviating many US citizens' concerns over the treatment of these individuals, however the SECDEF wants to categorize them.
The deletion of said video and photographic footage sends red flags flying throughout the ranks of active duty military, veterans such as myself, and the citizenry whose faith in this administration has fallen to record lows.
Birg. Gen. Watkins has penned a much more recent article in conjunction with Geoffrey Corn and summarizes the legality of the recent boat strikes as such, "While not discounting the harmful or deadly effect of illegal narcotics, chemical substances, even when illegally introduced into the United States, do not amount to an _armed attack_ the way flying commercial airliners into skyscrapers packed with civilians did. It is its use as a weapon to conduct an attack -- meaning an _intentional_ act of violence -- that matters. And that intent and corresponding attack is absent in this situation."4
Defining the nature of the conflict is key to defining the nature of the individuals involved; asserting the position that the United States is under imminent and dire threat of hostile assault allows this administration to assert the position that the individuals participating in that assault are combatants, and as we are not in a Congressionally-sanctioned conflict with them, the administration believes they can quite conveniently categorize them as "Unprivileged Belligerents" who are due absolutely no humanitarian protections under the Geneva Convention.
This US Navy veteran begs to differ. Transparency is the antidote to corruption. Transparency is the vehicle to accountability.
Remove transparency, and the foundation of an unbiased, apolitical standing armed forces crumbles beneath our feet, and we are left with a rogue military serving men and agendas instead of the citizenry as a whole. And _that_ is the most ominous threat imaginable.
1

subject: They're praying you stay silent...

body:
Trump’s asking, Are you an American Citizen or an Illegal Alien??
| | | | [](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)
---
Democrats are fiddling with their fingers, twisting their hair, and clenching their jaws as they pray you won’t respond to my message if this is true...

## **_MESSAGE FROM TRUMP:_**
|
---
| Look, I reached out to you last week to see if you would take my Citizens Only Survey.
Your file says you’re a top MAGA patriot, but I’m starting to wonder if that was true after all.
I cannot think of any reason that you wouldn’t have responded, unless...

### YOU’RE AN ILLEGAL ALIEN.

But I’m still clinging to hope that you’ll prove me wrong.
**So, tell me Friend —**
---
|
---
### Are you an American Citizen or an Illegal Alien?

[TAKE CITIZENS ONLY SURVEY](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)
\- DJT

name: Tobias Read
subject: For this administration, the bar is pretty low
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=251206em [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=251206em]
This is Oregon Secretary of State, Tobias Read.
Let’s be absolutely clear about what’s happening right now.
President Trump’s administration is threatening to rip food away from kids, seniors, and working families in Oregon — all because he wants revenge on Democratic states.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=251206em [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=251206em]
This isn’t governing. This isn’t leadership. This is cruelty.
The Trump administration is demanding that Oregon hand over private data on SNAP recipients, including names and immigration status, or else it will withhold food assistance from the people who need it most.
But Oregon said no. We stood up, followed the law, and defended our people. So now, Trump is threatening to starve families into submission.
I have news for President Trump: We will not back done or give in. We will not let your cruelty erode our democracy and our values. But I need your help to keep up the fight against Donald Trump at this perilous moment, so please: Will you chip in $5, $10, or more right now to help us continue communicating with Oregonians? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=251206em]
DONATE NOW [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=251206em]
Using Americans as political pawns is low, but for this administration, the bar is pretty darn low. hell.
That’s why I’m not afraid to stand up to them, call out their disgraceful political games, and defend our state from their cruel attacks with all my strength.
Because when Trump threatens Oregonians, tries to bully our state into betraying our values, and holds food assistance hostage, it’s up to us to protect our elections, our data privacy, and our democracy itself.
But I’m up against an administration willing to break every rule and hurt every vulnerable family just to score a political hit. And they are counting on states like ours being too afraid to fight back. That’s why I need you in my corner — $10 or more at a time — to help me win this fight for the people of Oregon. Can I count on you? >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=251206em]

clean_body: [] This is Oregon Secretary of State, Tobias Read. Let’s be absolutely clear about what’s happening right now. President Trump’s administration is threatening to rip food away from kids, seniors, and working families in Oregon — all because he wants revenge on Democratic states. [] This isn’t governing. This isn’t leadership. This is cruelty. The Trump administration is demanding that Oregon hand over private data on SNAP recipients, including names and immigration status, or else it will withhold food assistance from the people who need it most. But Oregon said no. We stood up, followed the law, and defended our people. So now, Trump is threatening to starve families into submission. I have news for President Trump: We will not back done or give in. We will not let your cruelty erode our democracy and our values. But I need your help to keep up the fight against Donald Trump at this perilous moment, so please: Will you chip in $5, $10, or more right now to help us continue communicating with Oregonians? [] DONATE NOW [] Using Americans as political pawns is low, but for this administration, the bar is pretty darn low. hell. That’s why I’m not afraid to stand up to them, call out their disgraceful political games, and defend our state from their cruel attacks with all my strength. Because when Trump threatens Oregonians, tries to bully our state into betraying our values, and holds food assistance hostage, it’s up to us to protect our elections, our data privacy, and our democracy itself. But I’m up against an administration willing to break every rule and hurt every vulnerable family just to score a political hit. And they are counting on states like ours being too afraid to fight back. That’s why I need you in my corner — $10 or more at a time — to help me win this fight for the people of Oregon. Can I count on you? >> [] DONATE NOW [] Thank you, Tobias Read [] [] [] [] Friends of Tobias Read (5208) PO Box 42307 Portland, OR 97242 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: .

name: Marsha Blackburn
subject: I owe you big time, Peter!
body:
I know my team and I have been reaching out a lot lately.

| | | | Peter, it’s Marsha Blackburn!
